title: Shift Leader
description:
Mandarin Oriental, Geneva is looking for a Shift Leader to join our Front Office Team.

Are you a master of your craft? Do you thrive in a team that succeeds together, demonstrating integrity and respect while acting responsibly? Do you embrace a growth mindset?

We invite you to become a Fan of the Exceptional.

Mandarin Oriental, Geneva

Mandarin Oriental is the award-winning owner and operator of some of the most luxurious hotels, resorts, and residences located in prime destinations around the world, with a strong development pipeline. Increasingly recognized for creating some of the world’s most sought-after properties, the Group provides legendary service inspired by Asian heritage whilst representing the very cutting-edge of luxury experiences.

Mandarin Oriental, Geneva, ideally located on the banks of the Rhône River, is a true landmark combining timeless Swiss elegance with Mandarin Oriental’s renowned hospitality. With 181 rooms and suites, exceptional restaurants, and a passionate team of professionals, the hotel embodies our mission to transform the ordinary into the exceptional every single day.

About the Job

Based at Mandarin Oriental, Geneva, the Receptionist reports directly to the Front Office Manager.

As a Shift Leader, you will be responsible for :

  • Monitor departures and arrivals on a daily basis.
  • Keep the booking schedule and occupancy schedule up to date.
  • Process bookings made by telephone, letter or email immediately and professionally within 24 hours of the booking being made.
  • Organize guest arrivals by printing registration cards, preparing group folders, etc.
  • Check in guests according to established procedures
  • Welcome and serve guests according to the hospitality standards of a 5-star hotel, in line with the expectations of the Mandarin Oriental Group and Swiss Deluxe
  • Comply with guarantee procedures by ensuring that we have cover for guests to be sent on credit (credit card authorization or cash deposit).
  • Organize guest departures (billing and room release).
  • Process guest departures in accordance with established procedures.
  • Enforce guest safe procedures.
  • Perform all administrative tasks in accordance with the daily checklist for each shift.
  • May be required to work both day and night shifts, depending on operational needs.

As Shift Leader we expect from you:

  • Previous experience in Front Office.
  • Team spirit, curiosity, and a desire to learn and grow.
  • Good communication skills and a flexible attitude.
  • Valid Swiss work permit or European passport.

Our Commitment to You

• Learning & Development – Your success is our success. We craft unique learning and development programs for every stage of your career so that you continue to grow and thrive.

• MOstay – When you work as hard as our colleagues do, it’s important to take time off. As a member of the #MOfamily, you can enjoy complimentary stays and special rates for you and your loved ones at our hotels around the world.

• Health & Colleague Wellness – Finding the right work-life balance is essential. Your wellbeing matters to us — that’s why we offer health benefits and wellness programs globally.

Local Benefits at Mandarin Oriental, Geneva

• A competitive salary and benefits package in line with the Swiss luxury hospitality market.

• Daily meals provided at our staff restaurant.

• Uniform provided and maintained by the hotel.

We’re Fans. Are you?

title: Sunset — Sales Development Representative, Data Partnerships
location: New York, New York, United States
description:
Sunset — Sales Development Representative, Data Partnerships

Type: Full-time | On-site | New York City (Dumbo, Brooklyn), NY Compensation: $75K base / $135K OTE (uncapped) + meaningful equity Hiring count: 1 Visa sponsorship: None available Reports to: Founder (no name/LinkedIn in source)

About Sunset

Sunset was founded to help founders — starting by supporting startups through shutting down, then expanding into unlocking a new revenue stream for all types of businesses. The underlying insight: the data a company generates day to day (collaboration, communication, building) is some of the most valuable training data in the world, and the next generation of model progress depends on real, proprietary data grounded in how actual businesses operate. Sunset is a primary source of it, partnering directly with the frontier AI labs. It has scaled from $0 to a multi-million run rate in a matter of months (multiple million in revenue in a single recent month), works with essentially all frontier labs, frontier data labelers, and RL environment teams, and is more than doubling its team right now.

Founded: 2023 | Team size: 11–50 (Seed) | Total funding: Not disclosed (recently raised again) Backers: Floodgate, Afore, Ludlow, Hustle Fund Industry: Legal Tech (per Contrario tag; operates as an AI-training-data business) Website: sunsethq.com Office: Dumbo, Brooklyn, NY

Why Candidates Should Join

  • Ground-floor seat: Category-defining business that scaled $0 multi-million run rate in months, with a direct reporting line to the founder.
  • Top-tier backing, real traction: Backed by Floodgate, Afore, Ludlow, and Hustle Fund; recently raised again; team more than doubling.
  • Frontier access: Partners directly with essentially all frontier AI labs, data labelers, and RL environment teams.
  • Uncapped upside: $75K base + uncapped variable at $250/qualified meeting $135K OTE, plus meaningful equity.

Intake Call Summary

Not present in source — the role page includes an Intake Video only, with no transcript or written summary. Do not fabricate.

The Role

The first point of contact for operating companies exploring a new data-licensing revenue stream, building qualified pipeline for the Data Partnerships Account Executives. This is the harder of the two SDR seats: outreach targets healthy, ongoing companies with no urgent trigger pushing them toward yes, so persistence and credibility matter more than in a typical SDR role.

What You'll Be Doing

  • Consistently hit or exceed monthly targets for qualified meetings booked
  • Generate a high volume of opportunities through personalized outreach to operating companies
  • Partner closely with Account Executives on sourcing, strategy, and deal handoff
  • Continuously find ways to improve and iterate on outreach and process
  • Speak credibly to the data-licensing and de-identification angle when it comes up

Tech stack: Not applicable (sales role).

Requirements

  • Hit or exceed the monthly goal of 20 qualified meetings booked, generating a high volume of opportunities through personalized outreach.
  • Partner closely with Account Executives on sourcing, strategy, and deal handoff, continuously iterating to improve outreach and process.
  • Work in-person 5 days a week from the Dumbo office.
  • 12+ months of BDR experience or equivalent quota ownership in consulting, closing, or operations, with the technical sharpness to speak to the data and de-identification angle when needed.
  • Persist through rejection and maintain performance after hundreds of no's, approaching healthy, ongoing companies without urgent triggers, backed by high EQ/IQ to stay sharp and credible throughout.

Green Flags

  • 12+ months as an SDR with clear quota ownership, or a consulting / closing / operations background where they carried a number
  • Relentless without a forcing event: has succeeded selling into healthy, ongoing companies where there is no urgency pushing a yes
  • Technically credible enough to hold the data licensing and de-identification angle in a conversation
  • Demonstrated instinct to test, measure, and iterate on their own outreach
  • High-slope early-career operator excited by an in-person, high-intensity, ground-floor seat

Red Flags

  • Seeks a work-life-balance environment; the culture is high-intensity and expects candidates who want to work hard and get after it
  • Only effective when there is urgency or a trigger event; struggles to create pipeline with healthy, ongoing companies
  • Stereotypical hard-sell rep who lacks credibility or cannot speak to the data angle
  • Slows down or drops quality after a run of rejections
  • No quota ownership or client-facing performance signal to point to

Role Details

  • Base salary — $75,000
  • OTE — $135,000 (uncapped; $75K base + $60K variable at $250/qualified meeting, monthly goal 20)
  • Equity — Meaningful / competitive equity
  • On-site policy — In-person 5 days/week, Dumbo (Brooklyn) office
  • Visa sponsorship — None available
  • Employment type — Full-time
  • Location — New York City (Dumbo, Brooklyn), NY
  • Experience — 1+ years

Benefits & Perks

  • $135K OTE ($75K base + $60K variable; $250 per qualified meeting, uncapped, against a monthly goal of 20)
  • Meaningful equity
  • 100% covered medical, dental, and vision insurance
  • Unlimited PTO
  • Free gym and workout class memberships via Wellhub
  • $500 desk allowance

Screening Questions

Not present in source — no Screening Questions section on the role page. Do not fabricate.

Required Candidate Q&A (Contrario submission form)

Role-specific questions on the Contrario form — answer at submission, not screening-call questions.

  1. LinkedIn
  2. If you don't live in NYC, are you open to moving?
  3. This role is onsite and in person. Are you willing to work from our Dumbo office Monday–Friday?

Interview Process

Durations and descriptions not provided in source; stage names carried from the role page. Contrario pipeline also includes admin stages "Pending Approval" and "Application Review" ahead of Initial Screen.

Stage 1 — Initial Screen — Initial screen. Stage 2 — Technical Interview — Technical interview. Stage 3 — Take Home — Take-home exercise. Stage 4 — Onsite — Onsite. Stage 5 — References — Reference checks. Stage 6 — Offer Extended Stage 7 — Candidate Hired — Candidate accepts and starts.

title: HR Generalist (3253)
location: CS San Antonio - San Antonio, TX 78217
description:
Human Resources Generalist

Full-Time | In-Person

Do you love to bring order and organization to projects? Do you have an eagle eye for detail? Do you thrive on keeping things running smoothly and enjoy solving problems? Are you excited by the idea of serving clients, supporting your team, and contributing to your community?

If this resonates, we want to know you at Clean Scapes. Clean Scapes is the largest commercial landscaping contractor based in Texas, with locations in Austin, Dallas, San Antonio, Fort Worth, and Houston. Our experts are committed to excellence, offering a variety of services from pre-construction and construction to maintenance and enhancements. Since our founding in 2005, the passion, reputation, and talent of our team has provided the opportunity to service some of the most prestigious and beautiful properties throughout Texas including the Oracle campus, Q2 Stadium, Omni PGA Frisco Resort, Hemisfair Park, and Waterloo Park.

We are seeking a Bilingual (Spanish/English) Recruiting Administrator to join our growing Talent Acquisition team. In this role, you will support the full-cycle recruiting process for our field employees, helping attract, hire, and onboard top talent while providing an exceptional candidate experience.

Role + Responsibilities

Full-Cycle Recruiting

  • Manage the recruiting process for field positions from application through onboarding.
  • Review applications, screen candidates, conduct phone interviews, and coordinate interviews with hiring managers.
  • Maintain an active pipeline of qualified candidates to support ongoing hiring needs.
  • Ensure timely communication with candidates throughout the hiring process.

Candidate Sourcing

  • Post and manage job advertisements across multiple recruiting platforms.
  • Source candidates through job boards, social media, community partnerships, employee referrals, and networking.
  • Proactively build talent pipelines for current and future hiring needs.

Hiring Events & Community Outreach

  • Plan, coordinate, and attend hiring events, job fairs, and community recruiting events.
  • Represent Clean Scapes professionally within the community and build relationships with workforce partners, schools, and community organizations.
  • Assist with creating recruiting materials, flyers, and other employment marketing initiatives.

Onboarding

  • Coordinate all pre-employment activities, including background checks, drug screenings, and new hire paperwork.
  • Facilitate new hire orientation and ensure employees complete onboarding requirements before their start date.
  • Partner with hiring managers to provide a seamless transition from candidate to employee.

Recruiting Administration

  • Maintain accurate candidate records within the Applicant Tracking System (ATS) and HRIS.
  • Track recruiting metrics and hiring activity to support department reporting.
  • Prepare offer letters and other recruiting documentation.
  • Support continuous improvement of recruiting processes and candidate experience.

Collaboration

  • Partner closely with hiring managers to understand staffing needs and hiring priorities.
  • Provide regular updates on recruiting activity, candidate pipelines, and hiring progress.
  • Support additional Talent Acquisition and HR initiatives as needed.

Education

While a degree is impressive, we recognize that what's on paper doesn't define you. Many of us have honed our craft through hands-on experience. We'll consider both as a foundation of talent.

  • Previous recruiting, staffing, human resources, customer service, or administrative experience preferred.
  • Experience recruiting for skilled trades, construction, landscaping, or field operations is a plus.
  • Experience working in a high-volume recruiting environment is preferred.

Knowledge, Skills, + Abilities

  • Bilingual (Spanish/English).
  • Passion for recruiting and connecting people with opportunities.
  • Strong interpersonal and relationship-building skills.
  • Excellent organization and time management skills.
  • Ability to manage multiple openings and priorities simultaneously.
  • Exceptional follow-up skills and attention to detail.
  • Comfortable working in a fast-paced, high-volume environment.
  • Strong communication skills with candidates, hiring managers, and community partners.
  • Self-motivated, resourceful, and proactive.
  • Proficient with Microsoft Office and applicant tracking systems.
  • Willingness to travel locally to hiring events and Clean Scapes locations as needed.

title: Physician - Neuro Otologist
description:
Inspire health. Serve with compassion. Be the difference.

Job Summary

The Prisma Health Physician is responsible for the provision of quality medical services to patients of Prisma Health-University Medical Group

Accountabilities

  • Engages in the diagnosis, prevention, or treatment of disease, defects or injuries and recommend or prescribe treatments for the relief or cure of physical, mental or functional ailments or defects.

  • Renders medical treatment to his or her patients consistent with generally accepted professional standards of care without regard to their ability to pay for such treatment and without regard to race, creed, color, sex, religion, national origin, or age.

  • Completes accurate, legible, and timely records with respect to all medical examinations and procedures; to accurately use Current Procedural Terminology and International Classification of Diseases codes.

  • Provides after hours call coverage equally with other physicians of the Practice unless there is an agreement otherwise.

  • Complies with standards of accepted medical practice, the rules and regulations of managed care organizations and other payors, including but not limited to Medicare and Medicaid (except to the extent those rules conflict with Physician’s professional medical judgment), and the standards of the Joint Commission on Accreditation of Healthcare Organizations.

  • Enhances clinical skills by maintaining sufficient continuing medical education to meet the requirements of the Physician’s certification and/or state licensing board.

  • Agrees to actively participate in non-revenue generating activities which serve to advance the Vision and Mission of Prisma Health. As an Academic Health Center, these activities may range from serving on committees, community outreach, helping to meet the teaching, the research goals and/or other activities as determined by the appropriate Department Chair. These important expectations are shared by all providers within Prisma Health–University Medical Group and are key elements of a high performing, integrated, physician led organization.

  • Participates in responding to requests for proposals for managed care contracts.

  • Participates in the establishment of quality assurance programs, utilization management programs, patient education services, and patient satisfaction programs.

  • Assists Employer in obtaining and maintaining any and all licenses, permits and other authorizations, plus achieving any applicable accreditation standards that relate to the business of Physician's Practice or Department.

Supervisory/Management Responsibility

This is a non-management job that will report to a supervisor, manager, director, or executive.

Minimum Requirements

  • Doctor of Medicine or Doctor of Osteopathic Medicine

  • Successful Completion of Residency; Board Certified or Board Eligible; Medical Staff Privileges

Required Certifications/Registrations/Licenses

  • Must be licensed to practice medicine in the state of employment

  • Controlled Substance License in state of employment

  • Drug Enforcement Agency Number

Other Required Skills and Experience

  • Basic Computer Skills

  • Knowledge of Office Equipment (fax/copier)

  • Mathematical skills

  • Specialty specific skills

Work Shift

Day (United States of America)

Location

9 Medical Park Rd Richland

Facility

3551 ENT-Midlands

Department

35511000 ENT-Midlands-Practice Operations

Share your talent with us! Our vision is simple: to transform healthcare for the benefits of the communities we serve. The transformation of healthcare requires talented individuals in every role here at Prisma Health.

title: Adult Reconstruction Surgeon - Warwick, RI
location: Ortho Rhode Island Warwick - Warwick, RI 02886
description:
Ortho Rhode Island (Ortho RI) has an exceptional opportunity for a fellowship-trained, Board Certified/Board Eligible Adult Reconstruction Surgeon to join its world-class team.

Founded in 2015, Ortho RI has grown into one of New England’s premier orthopedic groups. Headquartered in Warwick, Rhode Island, the practice’s 36 physicians provide comprehensive musculoskeletal care across a broad range of orthopedic subspecialties through six locations and a state-of-the-art ambulatory surgery center. Known for its clinical excellence and commitment to innovation, Ortho RI offers advanced technologies, including robotic-assisted surgery, minimally invasive nano arthroscopy, and orthobiologic therapies. The practice also provides comprehensive ancillary services, including advanced imaging, orthopedic urgent care, durable medical equipment, and physical and occupational therapy.

Highlights:

• Highly successful multi-specialty orthopedic group
• Newly created position to support practice growth and increased patient volume
• Call requirement with additional call pay
• Hospital affiliations include South County Hospital, Kent County Hospital, Fatima Hospital, Day Kimball Hospital and Ortho RI Surgery Center
• Clinical and administrative support
• Strong referral base
• Two-year partnership track
• Collegial work environment
• Patient centric culture
• ModMed EMR
• Robust compensation including guaranteed base salary with significant production-based bonus
• Comprehensive benefits package including relocation assistance and signing bonus

title: Special Education Paraprofessional
description:
At New Story Schools, we serve students ages 5–21 with autism and emotional, behavioral, and developmental challenges. Guided by individualized learning, therapeutic integration, and collaboration, we empower students to grow and prepare for meaningful adult lives. Join our team and help students write their new story.

As a Special Education Paraprofessional at New Story Schools, you will work alongside teachers to support students’ academic, behavioral, and daily living goals. You’ll play a vital role in helping each student succeed through direct support, patience, and encouragement.

What You’ll Need

  • High school diploma or equivalent

  • At least three months of experience supporting individuals with learning or behavioral disabilities

  • Knowledge of behavior management and classroom support strategies

  • Strong communication and teamwork skills

  • Ability to stay calm and consistent during challenging situations

What You’ll Do

  • Support students with academic, behavioral, and self-care goals

  • Implement IEPs and Positive Behavior Support Plans under teacher guidance

  • Assist students with transitions, activities, and personal care as needed

  • Collect and record behavioral and progress data

  • Maintain a safe, structured, and supportive learning environment

Why You’ll Love Working Here

  • Enjoy both paid time off and extra paid school breaks (for select roles), plus paid holidays

  • Wellness perks including gym discounts, mindfulness apps, and prescription savings

  • Tuition reimbursement, career development programs, and leadership training

  • 401(k) retirement savings with a 4% company match and immediate vesting

  • Health, dental, and vision insurance

  • Free Employee Assistance Program with confidential counseling, life coaching, and mental health resources

  • Life insurance, disability coverage, and Health Savings Account (HSA) contributions at no cost to you

New Story Schools is an equal opportunity employer, committed to diversity and inclusion in the workplace. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, pregnancy status, national origin, age, disability, genetic information, or status as a protected veteran, or any other characteristic protected by law. If you require reasonable accommodations during the application or interview process, please contact us at talent@newstory.com.

title: Chargé(e) du Développement Commercial
description:
Imaginez-vous...
  • Développer votre expertise et enrichir vos compétences à chaque projet.
  • Vous épanouir au sein d’une équipe bienveillante qui vous encourage à viser l’excellence.

C'est possible avec un rôle chez Linx Printing Technologies (www.linxglobal.com).

Linx, une entreprise Veralto, est un fournisseur mondial de solutions de codage et de marquage pour le codage de dates et de lots de produits et d'emballages dans les industries manufacturières. En tant que membre de l'équipe Linx et du réseau plus large de Veralto, vous travaillerez avec des produits qui ont un impact quotidien sur les aliments, les médicaments et les produits essentiels sur lesquels nous comptons tous. Vous aurez la chance de laisser votre empreinte et de réaliser vos ambitions avec de précieuses opportunités de croissance de carrière.

Chez Linx, nous savons que l'innovation et l'ingéniosité commencent par la priorité donnée à une main-d'œuvre diversifiée et à un lieu de travail profondément inclusif. Nous encourageons les candidatures de personnes issues de tous horizons

Si oui, lisez la suite !

Linx Printing Technologies est un fournisseur mondial d’imprimantes de codage et de marquage pour le codage des dates et des lots de produits et d'emballages dans les industries manufacturières. Vous ferez partie d'une organisation flexible et conviviale qui se soucie de ses employés autant que de l'environnement.

Dans le cadre d'un remplacement pour congé maladie, nous recherchons un(e) Chargé(e) du Développement Commercial est chargé(e) pour rejoindre notre équipe marketing pour un Contrat à Durée Déterminée d’une durée estimée à environ 4 mois.

Rattaché(e) à la Responsable Marketing, le/la Chargé(e) de Développement Commercial a pour mission de contacter nos clients actifs ainsi que nos prospects afin de générer des projets qualifiés, prêts à être convertis en ventes. A ce titre, il/elle joue un rôle clé dans la croissance de notre chiffre d’affaires en identifiant de nouvelles opportunités, en renforçant notre visibilité sur le marché et en suscitant l'intérêt de nouveaux clients.

Le poste est basé dans nos bureaux d’Evry Courcouronnes et est à pourvoir dans les meilleurs délais.

Dans ce rôle, une journée typique ressemblera à :

  • Exécuter des listes d'appels de campagne prédéfinies générées par le Responsable Marketing

  • Accroître le nombre de potentiels et la qualité de notre base de données clients (SalesForce.com)

  • Vos principaux indicateurs de performances portent notamment sur le nombre d'appels émis quotidiennement ainsi que sur le nombre de projets qualifiés générés.

Les exigences essentielles du poste comprennent :

  • Expérience confirmée en télévente

  • Succès démontré dans un environnement de vente sortante compétitif

  • Maîtrise des systèmes CRM, idéalement SalesForce et de l'intégrité des données

  • Anglais professionnel souhaité

Chez Linx, une entreprise Veralto, nous développons les talents ; nous vous donnons l'opportunité de développer votre carrière en fonction de vos forces. Linx est une destination de carrière pour les personnes engagées, passionnées et talentueuses qui sont motivées par l'innovation, la croissance et les opportunités.

Linx est fier de faire partie du segment Product Quality & Innovation de Veralto (NYSE : VLTO), un leader mondial de 5 milliards de dollars engagé à garantir l’accès à l’eau potable, à des aliments et médicaments sûrs, ainsi qu’à des biens essentiels de confiance. En rejoignant le réseau mondial dynamique de Veralto et ses 17 000 collaborateurs, vous intégrerez une culture et un environnement de travail uniques, où le sens rencontre les opportunités : un environnement dans lequel votre travail a un impact concret et quotidien sur les ressources et biens essentiels dont nous dépendons tous, et où vous bénéficierez de réelles opportunités pour développer vos compétences, poursuivre vos ambitions et faire évoluer votre carrière.

Ensemble, nous protégeons les ressources les plus vitales du monde™ — tout en construisant des carrières enrichissantes.

At Veralto, we value diversity and the existence of similarities and differences, both visible and not, found in our workforce, workplace and throughout the markets we serve. Our associates, customers and shareholders contribute unique and different perspectives as a result of these diverse attributes.

Unsolicited Assistance

We do not accept unsolicited assistance from any headhunters or recruitment firms for any of our job openings. All resumes or profiles submitted by search firms to any employee at any of the Veralto companies, in any form without a valid, signed search agreement in place for the specific position, approved by Talent Acquisition, will be deemed the sole property of Veralto and its companies. No fee will be paid in the event the candidate is hired by Veralto and its companies because of the unsolicited referral.

title: Senior Analyst, Service Excellence - Operations
location: (USA) Transitional Office AR BENTONVILLE Home Office
description:
Position Summary...

Role summary:
The Senior Analyst, Service Excellence plays a critical role in enhancing organizational performance by applying strategic frameworks and business insights to improve service delivery and stakeholder satisfaction. This position involves managing projects, analyzing business requirements, and driving continuous improvement initiatives to optimize processes and operational efficiency. The role requires collaboration with multiple stakeholders to resolve issues, support contract and vendor management, and ensure adherence to service-level agreements. The Senior Analyst contributes to advancing service quality and productivity while supporting the organization’s broader business objectives through data-driven decision-making and effective problem-solving.

About the team:
The Spark Earnings Excellence (SEE) Team ensures precise calculation, disbursement, and reporting of independent contractor earnings with a focus on compliance and data integrity. The team drives continuous improvement and operational rigor across complex processes, enhancing company performance and the global contractor experience. SEE collaborates closely with stakeholders to maintain strategic alignment and improve service delivery. Emphasizing leadership and cross-functional collaboration, the team implements effective solutions, upholds high standards, and supports ongoing process enhancements that contribute to organizational success and operational excellence.

What you'll do...

  • Analyze business data to support decision-making and improve organizational performance.
  • Collaborate with stakeholders to identify service issues and implement effective solutions.
  • Manage service delivery processes ensuring adherence to service-level agreements and quality standards.
  • Lead continuous improvement initiatives to enhance productivity and stakeholder experience.
  • Support project activities by tracking progress, managing milestones, and facilitating change management.
  • Evaluate contracts and vendor performance to mitigate risks and ensure compliance.
  • Develop and maintain knowledge resources to support service excellence and operational efficiency.


What you'll bring:

  • Proven experience in stakeholder management and service excellence within complex organizational environments.
  • Strong problem-solving skills utilizing diagnostic tools and methodologies to identify and resolve business challenges.
  • Demonstrated ability to manage projects, including tracking progress, meeting milestones, and supporting change management initiatives.
  • Knowledge of continuous improvement processes such as Kaizen or Six Sigma to enhance operational efficiency.
  • Understanding of business requirements across finance, operations, and service-level agreements to support decision-making.
  • Ability to collaborate effectively with cross-functional teams to implement solutions and improve service delivery.


At Walmart, we offer competitive pay as well as performance-based bonus awards and other great benefits for a happier mind, body, and wallet. Health benefits include medical, vision and dental coverage. Financial benefits include 401(k), stock purchase and company-paid life insurance. Paid time off benefits include PTO (including sick leave), parental leave, family care leave, bereavement, jury duty, and voting. Other benefits include short-term and long-term disability, company discounts, Military Leave Pay, adoption and surrogacy expense reimbursement, and more.

You will also receive PTO and/or PPTO that can be used for vacation, sick leave, holidays, or other purposes. The amount you receive depends on your job classification and length of employment. It will meet or exceed the requirements of paid sick leave laws, where applicable.

For information about PTO, see https://one.walmart.com/notices.

Live Better U is a Walmart-paid education benefit program for full-time and part-time associates in Walmart and Sam's Club facilities. Programs range from high school completion to bachelor's degrees, including English Language Learning and short-form certificates. Tuition, books, and fees are completely paid for by Walmart.

Eligibility requirements apply to some benefits and may depend on your job classification and length of employment. Benefits are subject to change and may be subject to a specific plan or program terms.

For information about benefits and eligibility, see One.Walmart.

title: CDL Driver - Roll Off - Shawnee
description:
The Roll-Off Driver will be responsible for safely, efficiently, and courteously providing waste removal services to customers. The driver will service industrial, residential, or recycle routes to mechanically collect construction debris, solid waste, or recyclables and transport to the post-collection facilities for proper disposal.



At GFL our goal is to invest in our people and provide opportunities to grow for life!

Our employees are affectionately known as team green, a name that unites us all under our shared purpose of providing sustainable solutions to enable our customers and communities to be GREEN FOR LIFE!

  • 15 days of paid time off.
  • 4 medical plan options including an HSA with employer contribution & match program, dental, and vision coverage.
  • 401(k) with an employer match.
  • Paid holidays.
  • Employee Assistance Program with free counseling services.

Overview:

GFL Environmental is one of North America’s leading waste management companies, proudly making communities cleaner, safer, and happier through first-class service. Working for GFL is more than a job — it’s an opportunity to grow in your career, make new friends, and make a difference each day.

Key Responsibilities:

  • Drives a roll-off truck along designated routes to mechanically collect, exchange, or deliver containers and take to the post-collection facilities.
  • Perform daily pre- and post-trip inspections; complete and submit a vehicle condition report at the end of the shift.
  • Courteously interacts with customers and/or the general public along the route to answer questions or respond to requests; Notify supervisor of issues requiring management action.
  • Clean loose trash off truck and/or ground around truck and container that spills during dumping process to maintain clean customer site and safety along roads and highways.
  • Maintains and submits a DOT logbook, daily route/productivity sheet, and vehicle condition report (post-trip inspection sheet) as required by law or company policy.
  • Maneuver truck into and out of minimum clearance spaces, using mirrors to back up.
  • Uses a key map and GPS to locate service addresses.
  • Follows safe operating practices, including lock out/ tag out procedures to ensure proper operation of the truck.
  • Monitors operations to detect loose debris, using mirrors or direct sight and/or listening for debris falling onto truck during dumping operation; visually scans customer site before and after dumping.
  • Adherence to all DOT, OSHA, FMCSA, and EPA regulations and standards.
  • Performs other job-related duties as assigned.

Requirements:

  • High school diploma or general education degree (GED) desired, but not required.
  • Possess valid Commercial Driver’s License (CDL).
  • Must be at least 21 years of age.
  • Minimum one (1) years of commercial driving experience OR CDL school certificate.
  • Must be able to meet relevant criteria for safety sensitive functions according to Company standards.
  • Must be able to demonstrate the ability to safely drive the truck and operate the equipment.
  • Experience operating a standard transmission on a commercial truck (depending on location).

Knowledge, Skills, and Abilities:

  • Knowledge of the truck and its components, and the basic maintenance required for safe operation.
  • Ability to control operations of equipment; maneuvering truck into and out of minimum clearance spaces, using mirrors to back distances of approximately 120 feet or more.
  • Ability to recognize unacceptable waste (such as gasoline or other flammable materials).
  • Ability to communicate effectively with internal and external customers.
  • Ability to read, write, and comprehend associated documents and maps.
  • Ability to understand and follow oral and written instructions.
  • Ability to follow all company safety policies and procedures.

Physical/Mental Demands:

  • Ability to stand, walk, use hands and fingers, reach, stoop, kneel, crouch, crawl, talk, hear, climb, balance, and smell.
  • Visual Requirements: include close vision, distance vision, color vision, peripheral vision, depth perception, and ability to adjust focus due to the constant maneuvering of vehicle in tight and high traffic areas.
  • Ability to regularly lift/move up to 10 pounds, frequently lift/move up to 25 pounds, and occasionally lift/move up to 100 pounds.
  • Sitting may be required up to 75% of the time due to the primary function of driving.
  • Ability to work in usually loud conditions.

Working Conditions:

  • Frequently exposed to loud noise, humidity, rainy/stormy, extreme hot and cold weather conditions (depending on season), moving mechanical parts, vibration, toxic and caustic chemicals, fumes, and airborne particles.
  • Work in outdoor environment up to 100% of the time.
  • Works route away from branch location on a continuous basis throughout the day.
  • Occasionally work in high precarious places.
  • Work in motor vehicle traffic conditions constantly.
  • Work environment is usually loud.

title: Regional Practice Manager, Construction, Projects & Assets - 12 month FTC
description:
Who We Are

FTI Consulting is the leading global expert firm for organizations facing crisis and transformation. We work with many of the world’s top multinational corporations, law firms, banks and private equity firms on their most important issues to deliver impact that makes a difference. From resolving disputes, navigating crises, managing risk and optimizing performance, our teams respond rapidly to dynamic and complex situations.

At FTI Consulting, you’ll work side-by side with leaders who have shaped history, helping solve the biggest challenges making headlines today. From day one, you’ll be an integral part of a focused team where you can make a real impact. You’ll be surrounded by an open, collaborative culture that embraces diversity, recognition, professional development and, most importantly, you.

Are you ready to make your impact?

About The Role

The Regional Practice Manager is a strategic and operational leader within FTI Consulting’s Construction, Projects & Assets (CP&A) practice. The role acts as a thought partner to regional leadership, translating strategy into execution, driving priority initiatives, and ensuring the effective operation and growth of the practice across EMEA. It is a delivery-oriented role requiring strong project management, analytical capability, and the ability to operate independently with senior stakeholders.

What You’ll Do

Strategy & Execution

  • Partner with EMEA Regional Leadership and Global CP&A leadership to shape the regional strategic agenda and translate priorities into executable initiatives.
  • Independently scope, plan, and deliver regional initiatives (e.g. business planning, operating rhythm, people programs, marketing and thought leadership execution).
  • Manage complex, cross-country initiatives with clear milestones, dependencies, and outcomes.

Business Planning & Commercial Management

  • Own and coordinate the regional business planning process -annual planning, forecasts, priority initiatives.
  • Track and analyse commercial performance including pipeline, pursuits, sold work, and utilisation.
  • Produce high-quality Excel-based analysis and PowerPoint materials for leadership decision-making.
  • Working closely with EMEA Resourcing and EMEA FLC Pricing leads

People & Talent Agenda

  • Support development and execution of the regional people plan, including recruitment, staffing, affiliation and engagement initiatives.
  • Coordinate closely with HR and Regional Leaders to support talent development and practice growth.

Marketing, Content & External Presence

  • Partner with FLC and CP&A Marketing to plan and execute regional thought leadership, newsletters, events and external visibility (e.g. SATT events)
  • Own regional CP&A content across internal platforms and external channels (e.g. website, FTI Perspectives).
  • Liaise with industry platforms, associations and sector initiatives (as required)

Stakeholder & Leadership Management

  • Operate confidently with Senior Managing Directors and Managing Directors across the region, along with the regional ELT.
  • Facilitate leadership meetings, drive agendas, track actions and ensure accountability.
  • Influence without authority in a complex matrix environment.

Operational Excellence

  • Identify and implement improvements to regional operating processes.
  • Act as a connector across global, regional, and functional teams to ensure alignment and execution.

How You’ll Grow

We are committed to investing and supporting you in your professional development and we have developed a range of programs focused on fostering leadership, growth and development opportunities. We aim to promote continuous learning and individual skills development through on-the-job learning, self-guided professional development courses and certifications. You’ll be assigned a dedicated coach to mentor, guide and support you through regular coaching sessions and serve as an advocate for your professional growth. As you progress through your career at FTI Consulting, we offer tailored programs for critical professional milestones to ensure you are prepared and empowered to take on your next role.

What You Will Need to Succeed

  • Strong project management capability (end-to-end ownership of complex initiatives)
  • Comfort operating with ambiguity and senior stakeholders
  • Proven ability to execute independently and drive outcomes

Basic Qualifications

  • Several years of relevant experience in consulting, professional services, or strategy/operations roles
  • Intermediate to advanced Excel (analysis, tracking, synthesis)
  • Strong PowerPoint skills (clear storytelling, executive-ready outputs)

#LI-CB1

title: Assistant Director of Engineering
description:
Based at Mandarin Oriental Punta Negra, Mallorca, as the Assistant Director of Engineering, you will support and assist in overseeing all engineering-related activities, ensuring that the services provided are balanced to meet the needs of the physical property, our customers, and colleagues. You will play an active role in the daily operations of the department, supporting the Director in leading, mentoring, and educating the engineering team. Your responsibilities will also include assisting in the financial management of property operations and maintenance expenses, as well as utilities consumption.

Duties and Responsibilities

§ Assist in maintaining the proper and safe condition of all buildings, plant rooms, equipment, and front and back of house areas.

§ Support the implementation of safety procedures such as Fire, Life, Health, Safety, and Security, Emergency, and Standard Operating Procedures in compliance with Mandarin Oriental Hotel Group standards.

§ Ensure compliance with relevant statutory requirements, regulations, and codes for all work carried out at the hotel.

§ Assist in emergency response duties as required, and act as part of the Emergency Response Team.

§ Assist in overseeing the activities of the Engineering Management Team under the Director's supervision.

§ Assist in the identification and coordination of outsourced engineering companies and services.

§ Participate in formulating strategic business plans for the hotel in collaboration with the Director of Engineering.

§ Help implement the hotel's community involvement strategies and assist in the development of community programs.

Assist in developing and executing preventative

maintenance programs according to Mandarin Oriental Hotel Group guidelines.

§ Support in managing the annual CapEx plan and budget for all engineering aspects of the hotel.

§ Help oversee general projects related to hotel enhancements, including assisting with project planning, administration, implementation, and evaluation.

§ Assist the Financial Controller in monitoring the hotel's financial performance, including the annual budget, energy consumption, and contractor selection.

§ Support the People & Culture Team in recruitment, employee development programs, labor cost management, community initiatives, and employee evaluation and recognition.

§ Assist the IT&T Director in telecommunications upgrades, annual maintenance of contingency systems, and testing of emergency power systems as per Mandarin Oriental guidelines.

§ Provide support to the Operations team with training and demonstrations on various systems like key systems, audiovisual equipment, lighting, and temperature control systems.

§ Attend Repairs and Maintenance, Project, and Risk Management Committee meetings as required.

§ Conduct regular inspections of all front and back of house areas to ensure quality and standards are maintained.

§ Ensure all technical documents, drawings, and manuals are updated in case of changes.

§ Maintain up-to-date certifications, test reports, inspections, and maintenance records in a well-organized manner.

§ Be fully familiar with all technical details of the hotel’s plant and services to provide support and ensure standards are upheld.

§ Assist engineering personnel with technical advice to ensure the maintenance of high standards.

§ Assist in preparing budgets, forecasts, and cost estimates related to engineering functions.

§ Actively contributes to energy conservation efforts and assist with energy control measures.

§ Maintain contact with local authorities and government departments (Fire Service, Electrical and Mechanical Service, Water Supply, etc.) to ensure compliance and to seek advice or assistance when necessary.

§ Promote and support MOHG’s Fire, Life, Health, Safety, and Security guidelines across all relevant areas.

title: Capture Manager
description:
Company Overview

We are a world-class team of professionals who deliver next generation technology and products in robotic and autonomous platforms, ground, soldier, and maritime systems in 50+ locations world-wide. Much of our work contributes to innovative research in the fields of sensor science, signal processing, data fusion, artificial intelligence (AI), machine learning (ML), and augmented reality (AR).

QinetiQ US’s dedicated experts in defense, aerospace, security, and related fields all work together to explore new ways of protecting the American Warfighter, Security Forces, and Allies. Being a part of QinetiQ US means being central to the safety and security of the world around us. Partnering with our customers, we help save lives; reduce risks to society; and maintain the global infrastructure on which we all depend.

Why Join QinetiQ US?

If you have the courage to take on a wide variety of complex challenges, then you will experience a unique working environment where innovative teams blend different perspectives, disciplines, and technologies to discover new ways of solving complex problems. In our diverse and inclusive environment, you can be authentic, feel valued, be respected, and realize your full potential. QinetiQ US will support you with workplace flexibility, a commitment to the health and well-being of you and your family and provide opportunities to work with a purpose. We are committed to supporting your success in both your professional and personal lives.

Position Overview

QinetiQ US seeks a Capture Manager with a demonstrable record of competing for and winning complex deals.

Candidates should be well versed in the Federal Acquisition Regulation, execution of captures using a multi-phase framework, and able to lead complex tasks including solution development. The ideal candidate possesses a portfolio of wins, can-do attitude, curiosity, and a desire to lead effectively and kindly in a fast-paced environment.

The Capture Manager will lead pursuits from qualification through submission. The candidate will report to the Chief Growth Officer and will work closely with senior leaders across the enterprise to execute account growth strategies.

Responsibilities

  • Lead all aspects of opportunity capture, post qualification phase through proposal delivery.
  • Coordinate with stakeholders in the line, business development, proposals, contracts, pricing, prepare, present, and mature opportunities through defined acquisition framework
  • Develop and execute customer call plans to define opportunity and timeline and validate our solution.
  • Engage with industry, assess the competitive landscape, and if needed secure teammates to bolster the QinetiQ solution.
  • Lead the development of win themes, technical and management solutions, pricing strategies, and risk mitigation strategies.
  • Interpretation of solicitation requirements, terms, and conditions
  • Meet or exceed established performance goals in support of overall corporate growth and success.

Required Qualifications

  • Requires a minimum of 7 years of demonstrated success in capturing $50M and above.
  • Demonstrable success leading matrix, cross functional teams
  • Demonstrated Experience with Government Assisted Acquisitions

Preferred Qualifications

  • Advanced degree in Management, Information Technology, or relevant field
  • Active within relevant industry associations
  • Active TOP SECRET security clearance #qinetiqclearedjob

Pay Transparency

The salary range for this role is $135,000 - $175,000 USD. The salary range provided is a good faith estimate representative of all experience levels. QinetiQ US considers several factors when extending an offer, including but not limited to, the role, function and associated responsibilities, a candidate’s work experience, location, education/training, and key skills.

Company EEO Statement

Accessibility/Accommodation:

If because of a medical condition or disability you need a reasonable accommodation for any part of the employment process, please send an e-mail to staffing@us.QinetiQ.com or call (540) 658-2720 Opt. 1 and let us know the nature of your request and contact information.

QinetiQ US is an Equal Opportunity employer. All Qualified Applicants will receive equal consideration for employment without regard to race, age, color, religion, creed, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, or protected Veteran status.

title: Field Service Engineer - Miami
description:
Field Service Engineer – US Market

Exciting News: US Expansion

Linx Printing Technologies is launching direct sales and service operations in the United States! As part of our ambitious growth plans, we’re looking for experienced Field Service Engineers to support our growing customer base across the US. In this role, you’ll be essential to building our reputation for outstanding service, supporting our innovative coding and marking solutions, and ensuring customers receive the highest standards of technical support.

This is a unique chance to be part of an exciting launch, contribute to our success, and grow your career with a global leader in product identification technology.

Imagine Yourself…

  • Helping Linx grow by delivering excellent technical support and service to customers in your region
  • Building strong relationships with customers through reliable, professional service
  • Joining a company with a proven track record of success and an exciting future

Role Overview

Field Service Engineers are responsible for installing, servicing, repairing, and maintaining Linx’s full range of coding and marking products across the United States. You’ll work hands-on at customer sites, ensuring our solutions operate at their best and our customers remain satisfied.

This role requires regular travel in Texas including 2–3 nights away from home each week to support customer needs and scheduled service activity.

What You’ll Do

  • Install & Maintain Equipment: Set up, service, and repair coding and marking systems at customer sites
  • Respond to Breakdowns: Diagnose and fix issues quickly to keep customer operations running smoothly
  • Deliver Great Service: Build trust with customers through clear communication, professional support, and effective problem-solving
  • Travel to Customer Sites: Support customers primarily across Dallas and Houston, with 2–3 overnight stays per week
  • Handle Service Calls: Manage field service requests promptly and professionally
  • Document Work: Keep accurate records of service visits, repairs, and maintenance
  • Support Linx Solutions: Help customers get the most from their Linx equipment, ensuring high standards of performance and reliability

What You’ll Bring

You’re someone who thrives in a fast‑moving, hands‑on environment and enjoys being close to customers. You take pride in representing a brand professionally and building trust wherever you go.

You will:

  • Enjoy being part of an energetic, entrepreneurial team helping to launch and grow Linx’s direct presence in the US
  • Be confident talking to customers, explaining solutions clearly, and building strong working relationships
  • Enjoy solving problems, especially when situations are new, unfamiliar, or technically challenging
  • Stay calm and effective when working under pressure or responding to urgent customer issues
  • Be highly self‑motivated, organised, and comfortable managing your own schedule and travel
  • Take ownership of issues and see them through to resolution, keeping customers informed along the way
  • Communicate clearly and professionally, both face‑to‑face and in writing

Your Background

  • Experience in a hands‑on technical, service, engineering, or customer‑facing field role
  • A strong service mindset with a genuine desire to help customers succeed
  • Comfort working independently, often on the road, as part of a growing US operation
  • Valid US driver’s licence
  • Willingness to travel regularly between Dallas, Houston, Austin and San Antonio including 2–3 nights away per week

About Linx and Veralto

At Linx, a Veralto Company, we grow talent; we give you the opportunity to develop your career based on your strengths. Linx is a career destination for engaged, passionate, and talented people who are driven to seek innovation, growth, and opportunity.

Linx is proud to be a Product Quality & Innovation company in Veralto (NYSE: VLTO). Imagine a world where everyone has access to clean water, safe food and medicine, and trusted essential goods. That is the tomorrow Veralto is creating today. Veralto is a $5B global leader in essential technology solutions made up of over 16,000 associates across our Water Quality and Product Identification segments, all united by a powerful purpose: Safeguarding the World’s Most Vital Resources.

US ONLY:

title: Registered Nurse-Neuro Telemetry Part Time Days
description:
The Staff Nurse Inpatient reflects the mission, vision, and values of NM, adheres to the organization’s Code of Ethics and Corporate Compliance Program, and complies with all relevant policies, procedures, guidelines and all other regulatory and accreditation standards.

A clinically competent, registered, professional nurse who prescribes, delegates and coordinates nursing care to the acutely ill patient through the nursing process of assessment, planning, intervention, implementation and evaluation. Effectively interacts with patient, significant others, and other health team members, while maintaining ANA standards of professional nursing practice.

Responsibilities:

  • Collects and analyzes data, identifies expected outcomes, develops and implements an individualized plan, coordinates care delivery and evaluates progress toward attainment of outcomes.
  • Directs care and determines the appropriate utilization of any assistant involved in providing direct patient care.
  • Collaborates with patient, family, physicians and ancillary staff. Creates a documented plan, focused on outcomes and decisions related to the care and delivery of services that indicates communications with patients, families, and others.
  • Participates in shared governance, committee work, and unit projects.
  • Evaluates and documents the patient’s responses to nursing interventions in the health record and properly individualizes the plan of care based on the evaluation of findings.
  • Utilizes the best available evidence, including research findings to guide practice decisions.

Required:

  • Bachelor of Science in Nursing (BSN)
  • Current State of Illinois Registered Professional Nurse
  • American Heart Association Basic Life Support (BLS)
  • Strong interpersonal skills
  • Ability to collaborate with others
  • Strong analytic abilities

*Associate Degree Nursing (ADN) acceptable with commitment to obtain a BSN from an accredited program within three years of first day of employment in clinical nurse role.

Preferred:

  • Experience in specific clinical area
  • Additional Preferences for the Emergency Room and the Pediatric Emergency Room
  • Trauma Nurse Specialist (TNS)
  • Emergency Communications Registered Nurse
  • Certified Pediatric Nurse
  • Certified Emergency Nurse

Northwestern Medicine is an equal opportunity employer (disability, VETS) and does not discriminate in hiring or employment on the basis of age, sex, race, color, religion, national origin, gender identity, veteran status, disability, sexual orientation or any other protected status.

Background Check

Northwestern Medicine conducts a background check that includes criminal history on newly hired team members and, at times, internal transfers. If you are offered a position with us, you will be required to complete an authorization and disclosure form that gives Northwestern Medicine permission to run the background check. Results are evaluated on a case-by-case basis, and we follow all local, state, and federal laws, including the Illinois Health Care Worker Background Check Act.

Artificial Intelligence Disclosure

Artificial Intelligence (AI) tools may be used in some portions of the candidate review process for this position, however, all employment decisions will be made by a person.

Benefits

We offer a wide range of benefits that provide employees with tools and resources to improve their physical, emotional, and financial well-being while providing protection for unexpected life events. Please visit our Benefits section to learn more.

Sign-on Bonus Eligibility (if sign-on bonus offered for position): Internal employees and rehires who left Northwestern Medicine within 1 year are not eligible for the sign on bonus. Exception: New graduate internal employees seeking their first licensed clinical position at NM may be eligible depending upon the job family.

title: (CAN) Préposé au traitement des commandes OMNI
description:
Position Summary...

Working as an OMNI Customer Fulfillment Associate at Walmart Canada is a great opportunity to build valuable skills in a fast‑paced retail environment. In this role, you pick, pack, and dispense online orders with a strong focus on accuracy, quality, and customer satisfaction, while following safety and food hygiene standards. By helping deliver a seamless online shopping experience, you play an important role in building customer trust and loyalty. This position is well suited for individuals seeking an engaging role in customer service or retail.

What you'll do...

WHAT YOU'LL DO

Pick, Pack, and Prepare Customer Orders
Picks and assembles customer orders from different temperature areas with care and accuracy to support on-time delivery. Packs orders carefully by confirming product selection, quantities, and product codes.

Ensure Product Quality and Order Accuracy
Selects fresh, high-quality products and removes damaged or unsuitable items. Completes all required documentation and labels accurately, including pick slips and shipping labels.

Maintain Efficient and Organized Packing
Packs totes efficiently to make the best use of space while maintaining product quality and safety standards.

Support Customers and Team Operations
Balances fulfillment tasks with positive customer interaction, providing assistance and helping resolve issues when needed.

Follow Safety, Food Hygiene, and Quality Standards
Maintains a clean and safe work area by following food hygiene and safety procedures, cleaning spills promptly, and ensuring totes are clean before use. Operates equipment safely and completes required safety checks. Demonstrates flexibility and contributes ideas to improve processes, productivity, and customer satisfaction.

Flexibility (Availability/Work Schedule)
• Be open to working a variety of shifts, including evenings, weekends, and holidays, based on business needs. Your adaptability helps ensure the store runs smoothly and customers receive consistent, high-quality service.

POTENTIAL CHALLENGES IN THIS ROLE

High Task Volume in a Fast-Paced Environment
• Manages overnight hours effectively and planning accordingly to stay energized and focused.
• Effectively manages work hours and plans ahead to maintain energy and focus throughout the shift.
• Handles a fast-paced workload with efficiency, ensuring large volumes of restocking and recovery are completed accurately and on time.

Physical Demands
• Performs physical tasks such as standing for extended periods, bending, and lifting inventory, equipment, or supplies weighing up to 20 lbs., and occasionally up to 50 lbs.
• Performs repetitive tasks such as stocking, zoning, and organizing products with attention to detail and consistency to uphold store standards.

WHY YOU'LL ENJOY WORKING HERE

Energetic, Fast-Paced Environment
Thrive in a dynamic setting where every day brings something new.

Appreciation for Your Efforts
Your contributions are recognized and valued.

Career Growth Opportunities
Build your skills and advance your career with us.

Meaningful Impact
Take pride in helping customers save money and live better.

The minimum rate of pay for this position starts from $18.75.

title: Team Manager - San Antonio
description:
Overview

The Team Manager will support the Director in leading, developing and supporting the sales, operations and security team members to meet and/or exceed commercial target and elevate the Tiffany store experience. You will assume oversight for the store when the Director is not present. You will be dynamic, attentive and an inspiring leader who builds relationships with internal and external clients; someone who could be called a mentor, a coach, and who acts as a Guest Experience Manager, ensuring unsurpassed client service.

As a Team Manager you will:

  • Effectively coach, guide and support the sales and/or operations of a Tiffany store to orchestrate the client experience that will lead to meeting and/or exceeding Commercial and profitability targets.
  • Be a strong, decisive, and collaborative leader who builds a climate of service excellence and leads the team to deliver extraordinary client experiences and partners with the Store Director.
  • Be a dynamic and inspiring leader who embodies the LVMH Values. You will Cultivate an entrepreneurial spirit, be Creative and innovative and Deliver Excellence in all you do. Whist at the heart of it all knowing People make the difference.

Sales

Deepen the relationship with your clients to achieve or exceed sales targets, product category targets, and relevant KPIs.

  • Manage and motivate the team to consistently achieve or exceed store commercial targets
  • Drive client development activities among individual team members to cultivate new and existing clients.
  • Demonstrate passion and deliver Tiffany Touch moments to both team members and clients, managing client relationships personally.
  • Drive business through key product pillars and KPIs.

Service

Execute in all things with a client-centric approach. Demonstrate passion and deliver Tiffany Touch moments to clients at every touchpoint:

  • Lead, model, and coach based on client feedback and elevate the Tiffany Experience.
  • Provide management presence on the sales floor, coaching the team and ensuring Tiffany client experience expectations are being always delivered.
  • Optimize hospitality and store amenities to create unique experiences.
  • Act on NPS performance and client feedback to improve customer service.

Talent

Attract, hire, and retain top talent to cultivate a climate of high performance. “People who like People”

  • Continuously train, coach, and provide qualitative feedback, utilizing reward and recognition as well as performance management process to improve team engagement and performance.
  • Prioritize diversity, cultivate inclusive environments, and foster growth
  • Encourage an entrepreneurial spirit
  • Set and communicate clear and challenging goals, aligned to our Strategic Priorities and Key Results.
  • Leverage and utilize training and development offerings to effectively support growth and development to drive performance.

Operational Excellence

Champion operations efficiency and effectiveness. Challenge the standards to seek continuous improvement.

  • Ensure exceptional operational support to drive sales and service.
  • Manage efficient back of house and ensure consistency with established operational procedures. Identify and execute efficiencies and best practices
  • Ensure compliance with all internal control procedures.

Experience

Required

  • Minimum of 3 years of retail or luxury retail store management experience or relevant customer related experience (e.g., hospitality).
  • Proven track record in sales generation, managing the achievement of commercial results.
  • Flexibility to work non-traditional hours, including days, nights, weekends, and holidays.
  • Proven ability to develop new opportunities and maintain client relationships while ensuring brand recognition and penetration in market.
  • Proficiency with Microsoft Office, Word, Excel, Outlook, Visio, Power Point, Client tracking systems and Point of Sales (POS) system.
  • Flexibility to work in various roles based on business needs (i.e., on the sales floor, operations, etc.).
  • Must have authorization to work in the United States or in the country where the position is based.

Desired:

  • A college/university degree.

title: Dialysis Nurse
description:
Why Work at Concerto? What is in it for you?

  • Three (3) day work week
  • Competitive pay
  • Nurse to patient’s ratio
  • Clinical/Corporate Career Advancement
  • Medical, dental, vision, short- and long-term disability benefits
  • Pet Insurance
  • 401k match of 50% up to 6% of salary
  • PTO
  • Paid Holidays
  • Discounts through Nectar
  • Employer Paid Employee Assistance Program

Who Are We?

Concerto Renal Services is an industry leader in sub-acute hemodialysis within a skilled nursing facility and/or long-term care facility. We are a rapidly expanding business with a simple mission: reimagining dialysis care, one community at a time. Our model focuses on ensuring those with end-stage renal disease receive the best possible care through a more convenient, centralized, continuous care model.

Why is this Role Essential?

Concerto is seeking a Dialysis Nurse to join its team! The Dialysis Nurse supervises dialysis care in accordance with policy, procedure and training and in compliance with state and federal regulations. Assures responsibility for the unit and works collaboratively with members of the hemodialysis healthcare team. Supervises care given by dialysis technicians while assuming a ‘hands on’ role and fostering a team approach to patient care.

What Will You Do?

  1. Patient Care & Education
    Provides direct hemodialysis care, performs assessments, administers medications, and educates patients on renal health, treatment, and self-care.
  2. Team Leadership & Supervision
    Delegates tasks to dialysis technicians, supervises their performance, and ensures compliance with clinical standards and regulations.
  3. Treatment Monitoring & Adjustment
    Monitors patient response to dialysis, adjusts treatment plans as needed, and ensures accurate documentation of interventions and outcomes.
  4. Collaboration & Communication
    Works closely with physicians and healthcare team members to coordinate care, report critical lab results, and ensure continuity of treatment.
  5. Documentation & Compliance
    Maintains accurate electronic medical records, ensures timely completion of care plans, and adheres to safety and regulatory protocols.

title: (USA) Principal, Software Engineer
location: (USA) Crossman Excellence Building CA SUNNYVALE Home Office
description:
Position Summary...

What you'll do...

Role summary:
As a Principal Software Engineer at Walmart, you will lead the design and delivery of scalable platform capabilities, including cloud-native architectures and AI/ML frameworks, to support millions of users. This role involves driving the full software development lifecycle, fostering technical excellence, and mentoring engineering teams. You will collaborate across functions to translate complex business needs into innovative technical solutions while ensuring system reliability and performance. Your leadership will advance Walmart’s engineering vision through strategic execution, continuous improvement, and knowledge sharing within a dynamic and evolving technology environment. **Immigration Sponsorship support will NOT be available for this
position**
About the team:
Walmart Data Ventures operates with the agility of a nimble startup, delivering best-in-class data products. The team developed Walmart Luminate, a platform providing actionable, customer-centric insights that empower CPGs and Walmart merchants to make informed, data-driven decisions efficiently. Focusing on technical strategy, reliability engineering, scalability, and cloud-native architecture, the group builds innovative solutions. Members engage in operational due diligence and continuous improvement, fostering a culture of learning and excellence to support Walmart’s commitment to delivering value through advanced data capabilities and cutting-edge technology.
What you'll do:
  • Lead platform engineering efforts to deliver scalable, reusable infrastructure and tooling aligned with strategic roadmaps.
  • Architect and implement cloud-native, distributed systems ensuring high availability, fault tolerance, and real-time data processing.
  • Drive full software development lifecycle across multiple teams, including coding, testing, deployment, and maintenance.
  • Integrate AI/ML capabilities into software products to enhance customer and associate experiences.
  • Provide technical leadership, mentorship, and guidance to engineering teams, promoting best practices and innovation.
  • Collaborate with stakeholders to define technical requirements and measure success through clear metrics.
  • Maintain architectural documentation and ensure alignment with long-term platform goals.

What you'll bring:
  • Extensive experience in platform engineering leadership, delivering scalable infrastructure and reusable tooling aligned with strategic roadmaps.
  • Proven expertise in cloud-native architecture and distributed system design ensuring high availability and fault tolerance.
  • Strong proficiency in software development lifecycle management, including CI/CD, testing, monitoring, and incident response.
  • Demonstrated ability to architect AI/ML integrated applications using languages such as Python, Java, and C++.
  • Skilled in applying model-driven design and leveraging advanced development tools to enhance velocity and quality.
  • Effective technical leadership and mentorship fostering engineering excellence and innovation.
  • Excellent collaboration and communication skills to engage cross-functional teams and stakeholders.
About Walmart Global Tech
Imagine working in an environment where one line of code can make life easier for hundreds of millions of people. That's what we do at Walmart Global Tech. Were a team of software engineers, data scientists, cybersecurity experts and service professionals within the worlds leading retailer who make an epic impact and are at the forefront of the next retail disruption. People are why we innovate, and people power our innovations. We are people-led and tech-empowered. We train our team in the skillsets of the future and bring in experts like you to help us grow. We have roles for those chasing their first opportunity as well as those looking for the opportunity that will define their career. Here, you can kickstart a great career in tech, gain new skills and experience for virtually every industry, or leverage your expertise to innovate at scale, impact millions and reimagine the future of retail.

Flexible, hybrid work:
We use a hybrid way of working that is primarily in office coupled with virtual when not onsite. Our campuses serve as a hub to enhance collaboration, bring us together for purpose and deliver on business needs. This approach helps us make quicker decisions, remove location barriers across our global team and be more flexible in our personal lives.

Benefits:
Benefits: Beyond our great compensation package, you can receive incentive awards for your performance. Other great perks include 401(k) match, stock purchase plan, paid maternity and parental leave, PTO, multiple health plans, and much more.

Equal Opportunity Employer:
Walmart, Inc. is an Equal Opportunity Employer – “ By Choice. We believe we are best equipped to help our associates, customers and the communities we serve live better when we really know them. That means understanding, respecting, and valuing unique styles, experiences, identities, ideas and opinions while being inclusive of all people.

The above information has been designed to indicate the general nature and level of work performed in the role. It is not designed to contain or be interpreted as a comprehensive inventory of all responsibilities and qualifications required of employees assigned to this job. The full Job Description can be made available as part of the hiring process. At Walmart, we offer competitive pay as well as performance-based bonus awards and other great benefits for a happier mind, body, and wallet. Health benefits include medical, vision and dental coverage. Financial benefits include 401(k), stock purchase and company-paid life insurance. Paid time off benefits include PTO (including sick leave), parental leave, family care leave, bereavement, jury duty, and voting. Other benefits include short-term and long-term disability, company discounts, Military Leave Pay, adoption and surrogacy expense reimbursement, and more. You will also receive PTO and/or PPTO that can be used for vacation, sick leave, holidays, or other purposes. The amount you receive depends on your job classification and length of employment. It will meet or exceed the requirements of paid sick leave laws, where applicable. For information about PTO, see https://one.walmart.com/notices. Live Better U is a Walmart-paid education benefit program for full-time and part-time associates in Walmart and Sam's Club facilities. Programs range from high school completion to bachelor's degrees, including English Language Learning and short-form certificates. Tuition, books, and fees are completely paid for by Walmart.
Eligibility requirements apply to some benefits and may depend on your job classification and length of employment. Benefits are subject to change and may be subject to a specific plan or program terms.
For information about benefits and eligibility, see One.Walmart.
