title: Senior Growth Marketing Manager | Cape Town
location: Cape Town, Western Cape, South Africa
description:
If running paid campaigns across a bunch of platforms is your happy place, and you think as much about the journey as the click, this could be a good fit. You're in Google Ads in the morning, LinkedIn Campaign Manager over lunch, and Reddit or TikTok in the afternoon. In between, you're thinking about how these campaigns work together to move real people through our funnel.

We're hiring a Senior Growth Marketing Manager to plan, deploy, and optimize campaigns across our paid channels. This role isn't just about setting campaigns live and watching the numbers. It's about thinking carefully about the marketing funnel, inventing new ways to bring quality leads and accounts into it, and using paid media to move them all the way through to sales-ready. You'll work closely with our Senior Director of Creative Performance Marketing, turning creative briefs and campaign strategies into live campaigns that actually perform. You'll own the technical craft of paid media (structure, targeting, bidding, testing, iteration) and the strategic craft of designing campaigns that do their job at each funnel stage.

This is an individual contributor role with no direct reports. What you'll be doing is deep, hands-on platform work combined with real strategic thinking about how paid fits into the broader journey.

Day to day, you'll:

• Plan campaign architecture across platforms with clear funnel intent (awareness, consideration, decision) and matching audience, creative, and CTA logic for each stage

• Get creative about lead acquisition itself: figure out how to reach new audiences, test new platforms and formats, and keep growing both the quantity and quality of leads coming into the funnel

• Design retargeting and lifecycle audiences that work with email nurture, content offers, and sales outreach to move leads through the funnel

• Partner with the rest of the marketing team on lead qualification and hand-off criteria, so what reaches sales is actually ready to convert

• Contribute creative direction to ad concepts and copy, working with our creative resources on work that resonates

• Build and launch campaigns in Google Ads, Microsoft Ads, LinkedIn, Meta (Facebook and Instagram), Reddit, TikTok, and emerging platforms like Chat GPT Ads

• Set up and manage audience targeting, including custom audiences, lookalikes, contextual, intent, and account-based lists

• Own bid strategy selection and budget pacing across campaigns

• Traffic creative assets, keep naming conventions consistent, and manage UTMs

• Set up conversion tracking (pixels, server-side, offline conversions) and make sure GA4 is telling us the truth

• Run structured A/B tests on copy, creative, audiences, and bidding

• Pull data, build reports, and share what's working (and what isn't) with the team

• Continuously optimize campaigns, moving budget toward what's performing and shutting off what isn't

What matters most

You should be a technically strong paid media operator with meaningful hands-on time across multiple platforms. Not just Google. Not just Meta. You should be genuinely comfortable in three or more, and hungry to learn new ones as they emerge.

Just as important: you should think in funnels, not just campaigns. The point of running paid isn't to hit CTR benchmarks in isolation. It's to move real people from not knowing us to considering us to choosing us. You'll design campaigns that work alongside nurture, content, and sales to do their actual job at each stage of the journey.

You'll also need to be a creative problem-solver about lead acquisition itself. Not just running the campaigns we've imagined, but figuring out how to reach audiences we haven't reached yet, inventing new ways to bring net new leads and accounts into the funnel, and partnering with the rest of marketing so those leads are qualified for sales when they hand off. Growing both the quantity and quality of leads in the pipeline is the real measure of success.

And the best paid media leads bring real creative instinct alongside the technical craft. They can look at ad copy and know why it will or won't land. They can spot the audience insight that turns a decent campaign into a great one. They have opinions about hooks, headlines, and offers.

That creative sensibility, on top of the technical skills, is what we're really looking for.

  • 5+ years of hands-on paid media experience across multiple platforms

  • Advanced expertise in Google Ads, LinkedIn Campaign Manager, and Meta Ads Manager

  • Working experience with at least two of: Reddit Ads, TikTok Ads Manager, ChatGPT Ads, YouTube, Microsoft Ads, programmatic, or other emerging platforms

  • Strong understanding of the marketing funnel and how paid media integrates with nurture, content, and sales to move leads through it

  • Real creative instinct: you have opinions about ad copy, hooks, and concepts, and can point to work you've influenced or written that performed

  • Creative problem-solving around lead acquisition: a track record of finding new ways to reach audiences and grow both the quantity and quality of leads in the funnel

  • Strong grip on conversion tracking (pixels, server-side, offline conversions, GA4)

  • Experience building custom audiences, lookalikes, and account-based targeting

  • Structured A/B testing experience and a track record of using test results to actually improve performance

  • Comfortable in dashboards and pulling your own data

  • Bachelor's degree

    Nice to have: experience with paid campaigns in multilingual markets. Localization, SaaS, professional services, or complex B2B global marketing experience. Hands-on with AI tools in performance workflows (creative variation, audience research, copy testing, predictive bidding, personalization). Familiarity with attribution modeling beyond last-click. Working knowledge of

    SEO, CRO, or organic social.

    A quick note on fit

    This role sits at the intersection of technical execution, funnel strategy, and creative problem-solving (both on the ad craft side and on the lead acquisition side). If you love the platforms, have real opinions about creative, think about paid as a way to move people through a journey, and enjoy the challenge of finding new ways to bring quality leads in, you'll thrive here. If you're purely a media buyer with no creative interest, or purely a creative with no platform depth, one side of this role will probably feel like a stretch. Applicants should be ready to talk through specific campaigns they've built, the creative behind them, how those campaigns fit into the funnel, and an example or two of creative problem-solving on lead acquisition.

title: (USA) Freight Flow Associate
description:

title: Nurse Technician - Ortho/Neuro | Part Time | Days or Nights
description:
At Singing River Health System, caring isn’t just part of the job — it’s in our DNA. Whether we’re saving lives, supporting behind the scenes, or showing up for our community, we bring the energy, heart, and hustle every single day.

With pride, passion, and commitment, we will improve health and save lives in our community every day.

Shift:

Days or Nights

Location:

Singing River Gulfport Hospital

Job Description Summary

Job Description

Position Overview:
The Nurse Technician provides fundamentals of nursing care (i.e. personal and restorative care) to patients according to predefined guidelines, a plan of care established by a Registered Nurse, and under the direct supervision of the Registered Nurse staff. Duties for this position will reflect the semester completed within the NLN. Expectation is for all performed duties to be in accordance with Singing River Health System procedures and policies, accreditation organization, and governing guidance and publications for health care employees.

DISCLAIMER: This is not necessarily an exhaustive list of all responsibilities, duties, skills, efforts, requirements or working conditions associated with the job. While this intends to be an accurate reflection of the current job, management reserves the right to revise the job or to require that other or different tasks performed as assigned.

Education:
Must be currently enrolled in an NLN School of Nursing. At completion of each semester Nurse Tech is required to provide new transcript to Human Resources.

Nurse Tech I – enrolled in a nursing program
Nurse Tech II – completed level I, enrolled in level II
Nurse Tech III – completed level II, enrolled in level III
Nurse Tech IV – completed level III, enrolled in level IV

License:
N/A

Certifications:
Must complete and maintain BCLS certification upon hire and/or transfer.
Must have de-escalation training completed by the end of position orientation (90 days); must have appropriate level of de-escalation training.

Experience:
None

Reports to:
RN responsible for patient care management/patient care during a shift, Patient Care Coordinator, Patient Care Manager

Supervises:
None

Physical Demands:
Work is moderately active: involves sitting with frequent requirements to move about the office, move about the facility, and to travel to another facility within the SRHS service area. Work involves exerting a negligible amount of force frequently to lift, carry, push, pull, or otherwise move objects, including the human body.

Work involves using many physical motions in performing daily work activities; subject to exposure of body fluids, sputum and tissues, which may carry the hazard of infectious disease. Work involves using repetitive motions: substantial movements of the wrists, hands, and or fingers while operating standard office equipment such as computer keyboard.

Work involves being able to perceive the nature of sound at normal speaking levels with or without correction; the ability to make fine discriminations in sound. Work requires close visual and acuity and the ability to adjust the eye to bring an object into sharp focus, i.e. shift gaze from viewing a computer monitor to forms/printed material that are closer to compare data at close vision.

Must be able to be active for extended periods of time without experiencing undue fatigue. Must be able to work schedules assigned with the understanding that changes may be instituted according to the needs of the hospital for off days, shifts or weekends.

Mental Demands:
Must demonstrate keen mental faculties/assessment and decision making abilities. Must demonstrate superior communication/speaking/enunciation skills to receive and give information in person and by telephone. Must demonstrate strong written and verbal communication skills. Must possess emotional stability conducive to dealing with high stress levels. Must demonstrate ability to work under pressure and meet deadlines.
Attention to detail and the ability to multi-task in complex situations is required. Must have the ability to maintain collaborative and respectable working relationships throughout SRHS and other organizations.

Special Demands:
Must possess superior customer service skills and professional etiquette. Must possess proficient knowledge and ability to use a computer (must be keyboard proficient) and other office technology (i.e., telephone, fax, etc.), MS Outlook and Word.

Must be able to demonstrate appropriate clinical judgment and apply appropriate professional skills to a patient population of all ages.

If you’re looking for purpose, teamwork, and a place where what you do truly matters, you’ve found it. Let’s do big things together. Apply now and join our team.

title: Chef
description:
As a Chef you’ll play a key part in the kitchen ensuring a busy service runs smoothly. You’ll make sure everything is well stocked, clean, and the team around you have everything they need to create the perfect dish for our customers.

As a Chef, you will…

  • Help the kitchen service run smoothly by making sure it is well prepped and clean
  • Maintain hygiene levels and safety regulations in the kitchen to help guarantee the safety of your entire team and visitors.
  • Communicate clearly with your team, ensuring they have everything they need.

What you’ll bring to the kitchen:

  • A positive can-do attitude to support your team.
  • A passion for challenges and thriving in a fast-paced kitchen.
  • Willingness to learn and expand your skills in the kitchen.
  • Sufficient spoken and written English to perform the role safely and understand allergen controls.

We’re all about rewarding our team’s hard work, that’s why…

You’ll receive a competitive salary, pension contribution as well as:

  • The chance to further your career across our well-known brands – as one of the industry's top apprenticeship providers, we can provide training and development at each level of your career.

  • Wagestream – access your wage before payday for when life happens.

CREATING PLACES WHERE PEOPLE FEEL WELCOME, SAFE AND THAT THEY BELONG

We aim to embed inclusion into every decision we make – across our people, customers, communities, suppliers, and operations. We want to create a culture where everyone feels welcome, valued, and able to thrive.

title: (IND) SENIOR, SOFTWARE ENGINEER
location: IN KA BANGALORE Home Office PTPP1
description:
Position Summary...

Walmart+ Membership’s mission is to build a best-in-class retail membership program that delivers unparalleled value, convenience, and savings to our customers. As one of Walmart’s strategic growth pillars, Walmart+ integrates benefits across delivery, fuel, streaming services etc to deepen customer engagement and drive long-term loyalty. Our team is focused on continuously evolving the membership experience through innovation, personalization, and seamless omnichannel integration.

As a Senior Software Engineer on the W+ Membership team, you will have the opportunity to work on high-impact initiatives that shape the future of customer loyalty at Walmart. You will lead the development of scalable platforms that power membership acquisition, retention, and engagement, while ensuring engineering excellence. As an experienced engineer, you will play a key role in defining technical strategy, driving cross-functional collaboration, and delivering customer-centric solutions. This is a high-visibility role with significant opportunities for growth and impact.

What you'll do...

About the team

Walmart+ Membership’s mission is to build a best-in-class retail membership program that delivers unparalleled value, convenience, and savings to our customers. As one of Walmart’s strategic growth pillars, Walmart+ integrates benefits across delivery, fuel, streaming services etc to deepen customer engagement and drive long-term loyalty. Our team is focused on continuously evolving the membership experience through innovation, personalization, and seamless omnichannel integration.

As a Senior Software Engineer on the W+ Membership team, you will have the opportunity to work on high-impact initiatives that shape the future of customer loyalty at Walmart. You will lead the development of scalable platforms that power membership acquisition, retention, and engagement, while ensuring engineering excellence. As an experienced engineer, you will play a key role in defining technical strategy, driving cross-functional collaboration, and delivering customer-centric solutions. This is a high-visibility role with significant opportunities for growth and impact.

What you'll do:

  • Responsible for the design, architecture, and end-to-end delivery of applications/feature , reusable libraries, and frameworks, with a strong emphasis on leveraging AI-powered tools for code generation, automation, and cross-platform development.
  • Raise the bar on best practices, code quality, documentation, testing, monitoring, and feature delivery by actively integrating AI-assisted coding, code review, and testing workflows into the engineering process.
  • Demonstrate strong communication skills to collaborate with stakeholders and cross-functional teams, including sharing AI-driven insights and recommendations for product and process improvements.
  • Champion sustainable engineering by continuously improving best practices, producing best-in-class code, documentation, testing, and monitoring, and adopting AI tools for productivity and quality assurance.
  • Estimate effort, identify risks (including those related to AI adoption), help with prioritization, and devise/meet project schedules.
  • Collaborate with other engineers, providing technical guidance on AI-powered development workflows, code generation, and cross-platform strategies.
  • Rise above details to spot broader issues, trends, and implications for the product/team, including the impact of AI and automation on engineering practices and team productivity

What you'll bring

  • BE/MS/Ph.D. in Computer Science or equivalent.
  • 6-9 years of strong design/development experience overall, with 6+ years of mobile development experience.
  • Deeply ingrained mobile-first design and responsive coding mindset, with demonstrated experience in building scalable, maintainable, and testable Android applications.
  • Expertise in Android SDK, SQLite Database, Kotlin, Coroutines, XML-JSON Parsing.
  • Good knowledge of location services, social networking integration (e.g., Facebook, Twitter).
  • Familiarity working with REST APIs for deep integrations, understanding best practices, shortcomings, and trade-offs.
  • Experience with offline storage, threading, and performance tuning.
  • Familiarity with cloud message APIs and push notifications.
  • Deep knowledge of Android UI design principles, patterns, and best practices.
  • Excellent organization, communication, and interpersonal skills.
  • Demonstrated understanding of AI concepts relevant to software engineering, including prompt engineering, code generation, and supervisory engineering (reviewing, correcting, and improving AI-generated code)
  • Experience using AI-powered tools for coding, code review, and testing within Android Studio or similar environments.
  • Ability to generate, adapt, and work on code across multiple platforms (Android, iOS, Web) using AI tools and cross-platform frameworks.

About Walmart Global Tech
Imagine working in an environment where one line of code can make life easier for hundreds of millions of people. That’s what we do at Walmart Global Tech. We’re a team of software engineers, data scientists, cybersecurity expert's and service professionals within the world’s leading retailer who make an epic impact and are at the forefront of the next retail disruption. People are why we innovate, and people power our innovations. We are people-led and tech-empowered.

We train our team in the skillsets of the future and bring in experts like you to help us grow. We have roles for those chasing their first opportunity as well as those looking for the opportunity that will define their career. Here, you can kickstart a great career in tech, gain new skills and experience for virtually every industry, or leverage your expertise to innovate at scale, impact millions and reimagine the future of retail.

Work
Walmart’s culture sets us apart, and we know being together helps us innovate, learn and grow great careers. This role is based in our [Bangalore/Chennai] office for daily work, with the flexibility for associates to manage their personal lives.

Benefits

Beyond our great compensation package, you can receive incentive awards for your performance. Other great perks include a host of best-in-class benefits maternity and parental leave, PTO, health benefits, and much more.

Belonging

We aim to create a culture where every associate feels valued for who they are, rooted in respect for the individual. Our goal is to foster a sense of belonging, to create opportunities for all our associates, customers and suppliers, and to be a Walmart for everyone.

At Walmart, our vision is "everyone included." By fostering a workplace culture where everyone is—and feels—included, everyone wins. Our associates and customers reflect the makeup of all 19 countries where we operate. By making Walmart a welcoming place where all people feel like they belong, we’re able to engage associates, strengthen our business, improve our ability to serve customers, and support the communities where we operate.

Equal Opportunity Employer

Walmart, Inc., is an Equal Opportunities Employer – By Choice. We believe we are best equipped to help our associates, customers and the communities we serve live better when we really know them. That means understanding, respecting and valuing unique styles, experiences, identities, ideas and opinions – while being inclusive of all people.

Option 1: Bachelor's degree in computer science, computer engineering, computer information systems, software engineering, or related area and 3 years’ experience in software engineering or related area.
Option 2: 5 years’ experience in software engineering or related area.

title: Director, AI and Innovation
description:
At OrthoVirginia, you’re part of a team dedicated to delivering expert orthopedic and therapy care across the state. As Virginia’s largest provider of musculoskeletal care, we offer full-time and part-time opportunities in a collaborative, team-oriented environment.

With more than 159 physicians in over 35 locations—including Lynchburg, Northern Virginia, Richmond, Southwest Virginia, and Hampton Roads—OrthoVirginia is a leader in orthopedic surgery, non-surgical care, and physical, hand, and occupational therapy. Our nationally recognized specialists treat a full range of musculoskeletal injuries and conditions, helping patients of all ages move, heal, and thrive.

Join us and become part of a trusted network committed to excellence in orthopedic care.

The Director, AI & Innovation leads OrthoVirginia’s enterprise approach to artificial intelligence and emerging technology, defining the roadmap, governance, and business case for AI adoption across clinical, revenue cycle, HR, and administrative functions. This role partners closely with IT application teams, clinical leadership, compliance, and vendors (including Epic, Workday, and Microsoft) to identify high-value AI and innovation opportunities, evaluate and pilot solutions, and scale successful initiatives responsibly and securely. The Director sets standards for responsible AI use in a healthcare setting and serves as the primary point of accountability for AI strategy, innovation pipeline, risk management, and measurable business impact, may build and lead a small team. ​

Key Responsibilities

  • Lead enterprise-wide data and AI governance strategy, roadmap, and accountability structures to align initiatives with clinical, financial, and operational priorities while ensuring compliant, ethical, and high-quality use of data and artificial intelligence.
  • Identify, evaluate, and prioritize AI use cases across the Epic (clinical and revenue cycle), Workday (HR and finance), Microsoft 365/Azure (productivity and data) ecosystems, and other AI solutions.
  • Lead evaluation and deployment of native AI capabilities within core platforms, including Epic’s AI/ambient documentation tools, Workday AI-driven HR/talent features, and Microsoft Copilot and Azure AI services.
  • Guides enterprise change management and data literacy initiatives to drive adoption of governance practices and responsible AI usage across the organization.
  • Establish AI governance, including a responsible-AI framework, model risk review, data privacy and HIPAA compliance safeguards, and vendor due diligence processes.
  • Partner with leadership team on key initiates to drive the business strategies across all functional areas of the organization identifying AI-enabled solutions with clear ROI.
  • Form relationships with Epic, Workday, Microsoft, and third-party AI vendors, including participation in user groups, roadmap reviews, and contract/licensing decisions.
  • Oversee pilot programs and change management for AI tools, ensuring clinician and staff adoption, training, and measurable outcomes.
  • Build, mentor, and lead a team of AI/analytics professionals; coordinate matrixed work with application analysts, data/BI, security, and compliance teams.
  • Monitor the regulatory and competitive landscape for healthcare AI, ensuring OrthoVirginia’s approach remains compliant and current.
  • Maintain a broader innovation pipeline — scouting emerging technologies and process innovations beyond AI that improve clinical or operational performance — and recommend which to pursue, pilot, or shelve.
  • Report AI and innovation program progress, risks, and outcomes to executive leadership as needed.

Required Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree in Information Technology, Computer Science, Data Science, Healthcare Administration, or related field (Master’s preferred).
  • 7+ years of progressive experience in healthcare IT, digital strategy, or data/analytics, including at least 3 years leading AI, automation, or advanced analytics initiatives.
  • Direct experience working within or alongside Epic, Workday, or Microsoft 365/Azure environments.
  • Experience with Epic modules such as Cognitive Computing Platform, Abridge ambient documentation, or Epic AI tools.
  • Demonstrated experience leading cross-functional technology initiatives and managing vendor relationships in a healthcare setting.
  • Working knowledge of HIPAA, data privacy, and AI governance/risk considerations in healthcare.
  • Prior people-management experience, with the ability to build and develop a team.
  • Strong executive communication skills, with the ability to translate technical AI concepts for clinical and business leaders.

Preferred Qualifications

  • Experience with Workday AI/ML features (skills-based talent, Workday Assistant) or Microsoft Copilot for Microsoft 365/Azure AI Foundry.
  • Certification or formal training in AI/ML, healthcare informatics (e.g., CPHIMS), or project/program management (PMP).
  • Experience in a multi-site ambulatory or specialty physician practice environment.

Technical Environment

This role works across OrthoVirginia’s core enterprise platforms: Epic (EHR, practice management, and revenue cycle), Workday (HCM and Financials), and the Microsoft ecosystem (Microsoft 365, Teams, Azure, Copilot). Familiarity with data platforms and reporting tools used alongside these systems (e.g., Azure Data Factory, Power BI, or Epic Clarity/Caboodle) is a plus.

Core Competencies

  • Strategic thinking with the ability to translate vision into an executable roadmap
  • Strong grasp of AI/ML fundamentals, responsible AI, and data governance
  • Collaborative leadership across clinical, operational, and technical stakeholders
  • Vendor and program management in a regulated, enterprise healthcare setting
  • Clear, confident communication with executives, clinicians, and staff

Work Environment & Physical Requirements

  • Remote-based role with periodic in-person travel to OrthoVirginia locations in Virginia for meetings, workshops, and go-live support
  • Standard office/home-office environment; prolonged use of a computer and virtual collaboration tools
  • Occasional travel to other OrthoVirginia practice sites as needed

Compensation Range for this position:

$163,070.00 - $244,605.00

Compensation for this role will be based on several factors, including the candidate's qualifications, relevant experience, education, skills, certifications, geography/market, and internal equity considerations.

title: (CAN) OMNI Customer Fulfillment Associate
description:
Position Summary...

Working as an OMNI Customer Fulfillment Associate at Walmart Canada is a great opportunity to build valuable skills in a fast‑paced retail environment. In this role, you pick, pack, and dispense online orders with a strong focus on accuracy, quality, and customer satisfaction, while following safety and food hygiene standards. By helping deliver a seamless online shopping experience, you play an important role in building customer trust and loyalty. This position is well suited for individuals seeking an engaging role in customer service or retail.

What you'll do...

WHAT YOU'LL DO

Pick, Pack, and Prepare Customer Orders
Picks and assembles customer orders from different temperature areas with care and accuracy to support on-time delivery. Packs orders carefully by confirming product selection, quantities, and product codes.

Ensure Product Quality and Order Accuracy
Selects fresh, high-quality products and removes damaged or unsuitable items. Completes all required documentation and labels accurately, including pick slips and shipping labels.

Maintain Efficient and Organized Packing
Packs totes efficiently to make the best use of space while maintaining product quality and safety standards.

Support Customers and Team Operations
Balances fulfillment tasks with positive customer interaction, providing assistance and helping resolve issues when needed.

Follow Safety, Food Hygiene, and Quality Standards
Maintains a clean and safe work area by following food hygiene and safety procedures, cleaning spills promptly, and ensuring totes are clean before use. Operates equipment safely and completes required safety checks. Demonstrates flexibility and contributes ideas to improve processes, productivity, and customer satisfaction.

Flexibility (Availability/Work Schedule)
• Be open to working a variety of shifts, including evenings, weekends, and holidays, based on business needs. Your adaptability helps ensure the store runs smoothly and customers receive consistent, high-quality service.

POTENTIAL CHALLENGES IN THIS ROLE

High Task Volume in a Fast-Paced Environment
• Manages overnight hours effectively and planning accordingly to stay energized and focused.
• Effectively manages work hours and plans ahead to maintain energy and focus throughout the shift.
• Handles a fast-paced workload with efficiency, ensuring large volumes of restocking and recovery are completed accurately and on time.

Physical Demands
• Performs physical tasks such as standing for extended periods, bending, and lifting inventory, equipment, or supplies weighing up to 20 lbs., and occasionally up to 50 lbs.
• Performs repetitive tasks such as stocking, zoning, and organizing products with attention to detail and consistency to uphold store standards.

WHY YOU'LL ENJOY WORKING HERE

Energetic, Fast-Paced Environment
Thrive in a dynamic setting where every day brings something new.

Appreciation for Your Efforts
Your contributions are recognized and valued.

Career Growth Opportunities
Build your skills and advance your career with us.

Meaningful Impact
Take pride in helping customers save money and live better.

The minimum rate of pay for this position starts from $17.75.

title: Senior Backend Engineer - Databases - Loki Ingest  | Germany | Remote
description:
Grafana Labs, the company behind the open observability cloud, is founded on the principles of open source, open standards, open ecosystems, and open culture. Grafana Cloud, our fully managed observability platform, is flexible and built for scale. With Grafana Cloud's actually useful AI, organizations can see, understand, and act on all their disparate data to move at the speed of their ambitions. Today, more than 35 million users and 7,000+ customers – including Anthropic, Bloomberg, NVIDIA, Microsoft, and Salesforce – trust Grafana Labs to ensure reliability of their applications and systems, resolve incidents quickly, and optimize their telemetry to reduce noise and cost. We are a 100% remote company with 1,600+ team members across 40+ countries, and we’re backed by leading investors including Lightspeed Venture Partners, Sequoia Capital, GIC, Coatue, J.P. Morgan, CapitalG, and Lead Edge Capital. Learn more at grafana.com and follow us on LinkedIn and X.

We’re scaling fast and staying true to what makes us different: an open-source legacy, a global collaborative culture, and a passion for meaningful work. Our team thrives in an innovation-driven environment where transparency, autonomy, and trust fuel everything we do.

You may not meet every requirement, and that’s okay. If this role excites you, we’d love you to raise your hand for what could be a truly career-defining opportunity.

This is a remote opportunity and we would be interested in applicants based in Spain, Ireland, Germany, the UK or Sweden at this time.

Senior Backend Engineer - Databases - Loki Ingest | EMEA | Remote

The Opportunity:

Grafana Cloud is our composable observability platform that integrates metrics, logs, and traces with Grafana. It allows our customers to leverage the best open source observability software – including Prometheus, Mimir, Loki, and Tempo – without the overhead of installing, maintaining and scaling their own observability stack

The Databases team owns the telemetry databases that are Mimir for metrics, Loki for logs, Tempo for traces, and Pyroscope for profiles. Our databases are OSS projects that we also offer as a Cloud service supporting Grafana Cloud, and as an on-premise solution. They are multi-tenant distributed systems implemented in Go and running on Kubernetes across all major Cloud service providers (GCP, Azure, AWS). We also have engineers working on Prometheus, Grafana Agent, Mimir proxies, and OpenTelemetry.

As a company we are remote-first and global, we embrace people of different experiences and backgrounds to build diverse teams where every person brings a new perspective to the software. Our tech stack is mostly made up of services written in Go, running on multiple Kubernetes clusters that leverage Cloud object storage.

We invest heavily in developer productivity. You can use modern AI coding assistants as part of your daily workflow (your choice of tools, within security guidelines), backed by a company-funded usage budget so you can iterate quickly without unnecessary friction.

We encourage pragmatic AI-assisted development: faster prototyping, test generation, refactors, documentation, and incident follow-ups—always paired with strong code review and quality standards.

You’ll also have access to frontier models (e.g., GPT-Codex 5/3, Claude Opus 4.6, Gemini 3 Pro).

What You’ll Be Doing:

  • Take an active role in influencing our roadmap and your own career objectives
  • Work with your team to deliver new features, then use the results to iterate and improve.
  • Drive projects from initial ideation all the way to operations once it is in the hands of customers
  • Embrace our open-source culture and contribute to other projects that may not directly fall within your team’s scope
  • Design, build, operate, and maintain critical systems, owning the reliability, performance, and availability
  • Be a part of your team’s on-call rotations and take ownership of the services you’re running
  • Supporting other team members, participate in design discussions and collaborate with the team
  • Learn new skills by gaining a deeper understanding of our cloud product and our customers and getting to know the codebase of a large distributed system

As we are remote-first and our engineering organization is largely remote, we provide guidance and meet regularly using video calls, so an independent attitude and good communication skills are a must.

What Makes You a Great Fit:

  • You are a motivated self starter with a bias towards action
  • You are customer focused. We build everything with our users in mind. You have a passion for creating intuitive products that fit customers’ needs
  • Pragmatism: You are able to take on complex challenges and break them down to achieve short feedback loops: to analyze, design, and build modular solutions, deliver MVPs, gather data and feedback and then progress iteratively
  • Collaboration and communication: The smallest unit we have is a team. You’ll be working with your teammates in a fully remote setup. Good communication skills are a must

Requirements:

  • Solid experience with at least one programming language. We use Go, but if you have familiarity with Python, C, C++, Rust or similar then that translates well
  • Some experience with delivering projects from gathering requirements, brainstorming ideas all the way to shipping a product to the customer’s hands in a self-driven way
  • Some experience with developing software that runs in the Cloud or some experience with systems engineering
  • Experience writing clean, robust, and performant software that is easily maintained by others

Bonus Points For:

  • Experience working with Kubernetes and/or Docker
  • Been a user of Grafana and Prometheus in operational roles (including on-call for your team at a previous employer or just using these tools on hobby/homelab projects)
  • Exposure to microservices architecture and distributed systems, and a desire to learn
  • Familiarity with being on-call and performing operations/SRE tasks or with the concept of infrastructure as code

Compensation & Rewards:

In Germany, the Base compensation range for this role is €97,000 - €121,000. Actual compensation may vary based on level, experience, and skillset as assessed in the interview process. Benefits include equity, bonus (if applicable) and other benefits listed here.

*Compensation ranges are country specific. If you are applying for this role from a different location than listed above, your recruiter will discuss your specific market’s defined pay range & benefits at the beginning of the process.

Why You’ll Thrive at Grafana Labs:

  • 100% Remote, Global Culture - As a remote-only company, we bring together talent from around the world, united by a culture of collaboration and shared purpose.
  • Scaling Organization – Tackle meaningful work in a high-growth, ever-evolving environment.
  • Transparent Communication – Expect open decision-making and regular company-wide updates.
  • Innovation-Driven – Autonomy and support to ship great work and try new things.
  • Open Source Roots – Built on community-driven values that shape how we work.
  • Empowered Teams – High trust, low ego culture that values outcomes over optics.
  • Career Growth Pathways – Defined opportunities to grow and develop your career.
  • Approachable Leadership – Transparent execs who are involved, visible, and human.
  • Passionate People – Join a team of smart, supportive folks who care deeply about what they do.
  • In-Person onboarding - We want you to thrive from day 1 with your fellow new ‘Grafanistas’ to learn all about what we do and how we do it.
  • Balance is Key - We operate a global annual leave policy of 30 days per annum. 3 days of your annual leave entitlement are reserved for Grafana Shutdown Days to allow the team to really disconnect. *We will comply with local legislation where applicable.

Equal Opportunity Employer: Grafana Labs is an equal opportunities employer. We welcome applications from everyone regardless of race, colour, nationality, origin, caste, sex, gender reassignment identity or expression, sexual orientation, age, religion or belief, disability, veteran status, genetic information, pregnancy, maternity, marital, family or carer status, or any other characteristic which is protected by local law. We believe that equality and diversity build a strong organisation, and we work hard to ensure that is the foundation of our organisation as we grow.

Grafana Labs may utilize AI tools in its recruitment process to assist in matching information provided in CVs to job postings. The recruitment team will continue to review inbound CVs manually to identify alignment with current openings.

#LI-Remote

For information about how your personal data is used once you’ve applied to a job, check out our privacy policy.

title: Team Lead, Paralegal
description:
An Exciting Update About Our Organization

Thank you for your interest in joining CLV at a truly exceptional moment in our organization’s history.

On July 9, 2026, CLV officially acquired InterRent REIT, bringing together two accomplished organizations, talented teams and a shared commitment to our people, residents, partners and communities across Canada.

Together, we are building something remarkable. We are growing at significant scale, advancing some of the most ambitious projects in our history and creating an organization where talented people can do meaningful work, learn from some of the best people in the industry and build careers with extraordinary potential.

Who We Are

For more than 50 years, CLV has acquired, developed and managed residential communities across Canada, earning a reputation for the quality of our work, the strength of our teams and our commitment to the communities we serve.


Today we are building one of Canada’s most dynamic and integrated real estate platforms, with the scale, talent and vision to deliver outstanding projects and make a meaningful impact across the country. Our work spans investments, acquisitions, development, asset management, property management and corporate services. That breadth creates opportunities for our people to contribute across the full real estate journey, work alongside some of Canada’s most exceptional talent and take on challenges that accelerate their growth.


This is an extraordinary time to join CLV. We are growing quickly, investing boldly and building for the future of our organization. We are looking for exceptional people who want to be part of something significant, contribute to work they are proud of and do some of the best work of their careers.

We are excited to learn more about you and the role you could play in what we are building together.


Learn more about CLV and apply here: https://www.clvgroup.com/about-us/

Learn more about our recent acquisition of InterRent REIT: HERE


About the Role
As we continue to grow and evolve, with an expanding residential portfolio and an increasing focus on operational excellence, we are strengthening our Legal and Resident Relations capabilities. We are seeking a Team Lead, Paralegal to oversee our resident legal function while providing day-to-day leadership and guidance to our Paralegal team.


This role is responsible for ensuring the timely, accurate, and consistent management of landlord tenant legal matters across our residential portfolio. In addition to managing a portfolio of complex legal files, the Team Lead will provide coaching and guidance to the Paralegal team, establish best practices, monitor workflow and quality, and partner closely with Property Management, Accounting, and Legal to deliver compliant, efficient, and resident-focused outcomes.


The ideal candidate is an experienced licensed Paralegal with strong leadership capabilities, exceptional organizational skills, and a passion for building efficient processes and developing people. This individual will represent the organization before the Landlord and Tenant Board (LTB), provide subject matter expertise on the Residential Tenancies Act, and help drive continuous improvement across the legal operations function.

Key Responsibilities
Leadership & Team Management

  • Provide day-to-day leadership, coaching, and mentorship to a team of Paralegals.
  • Review work for accuracy, quality, and compliance with legislation and internal standards.
  • Monitor workload distribution and prioritize files to ensure service levels are met.

  • Identify opportunities to improve workflows, increase efficiency, and standardize
    processes.
  • Act as the primary escalation point for complex resident legal matters.

Legal Operations

  • Oversee the preparation and filing of Landlord and Tenant Board (LTB) applications and
    prescribed legal documentation.
  • Maintain an active caseload, including preparing legal forms, reviewing files, and
    representing the organization at LTB hearings.
  • Review resident ledgers and supporting documentation to ensure accuracy and support
    collections efforts.
  • Review, interpret, and implement LTB Orders, mediated agreements, and related legal
    decisions.
  • Research legal issues, gather evidence, and oversee disclosure preparation for hearings.
  • Negotiate payment agreements and settlement arrangements where appropriate.
  • Ensure all legal files are organized, complete, and maintained in accordance with internal
    standards.

Collaboration & Compliance

  • Partner closely with Property Management, Accounting, and In-House Legal Counsel to resolve complex legal matters.
  • Provide guidance and support to internal stakeholders regarding landlord-tenant legislation and legal processes.
  • Monitor legislative changes affecting residential tenancy law and communicate updates to the team.


Qualifications
Must-Haves

  • Licensed P1 Paralegal in good standing with the Law Society of Ontario.
  • 5+ years of paralegal experience, with experience representing landlords before the Landlord and Tenant Board.
  • Strong working knowledge of the Residential Tenancies Act and related legislation.
  • Previous experience leading, mentoring, or coaching a team (formal leadership experience preferred).
  • Demonstrated ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment.
  • Excellent analytical, organizational, and problem-solving skills.
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills.
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Office.


Assets

  • Experience using Yardi or other property management software.
  • Experience supporting a large multi-residential property portfolio


Most importantly, we are a small yet mighty team. This is a roll up your sleeves, help where you are needed kind of role. We are looking for a new team member who will be a culture builder, someone who will lean in to help us build an engaged and positive work environment, and someone who will support our success when and as needed, as our team will do for you when you need it.


This role is based in our offices five days a week, giving you the opportunity to work closely with your colleagues, learn from experienced professionals, and grow in a highly engaged team environment. Travel will be required to hearings and meetings offsite.


Final compensation will be determined based on a variety of factors including skills, experience, educational attainment, professional designations, internal equity, and other job-related requirements. We recognize that individuals bring different skill sets, strengths, and career experiences, and we encourage candidates at all stages of their career to apply. If your experience or compensation expectations differ from the posted range, we still welcome your application, as we value potential, growth, and the unique contributions each person can bring to CLV.


Growth at this scale creates meaningful opportunity. Opportunity to bring teams together with purpose. Opportunity to continue building a high-performing, values driven organization that is equipped to serve residents and communities with care, consistency, and excellence. If this is the kind of opportunity that resonates with you, we look forward to hearing from you.

Our Commitment to an Inclusive and Accessible Hiring Process

We are committed to diversity in our workforce and are proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We are excited to work with talented people.

All employment decisions are based on business needs, job requirements, and individual qualifications, without regard to race, color, religion or belief, national or ethnic origin, gender, age, disability, sexual orientation, gender identity and/or expression, marital or civil status, political affiliation, family or parental status, or any other status protected by the laws or regulations in the jurisdictions in which we operate.


We strive to make our website and application process accessible to all users. Accommodation is available upon request for candidates taking part in all aspects of the selection process. Please send an email to hr@clvgroup.com with the nature of your request and your contact information. This email is for accessibility requests only and cannot be used to inquire about the status of applications.


Our team handles a lot of sensitive information, which means we require all candidates that receive and accept employment offers to complete a background check before being hired.


Only selected candidates will be contacted. No phone calls please.


We appreciate your interest in exploring a career with CLV!

title: EMPLOYE(E) COMMERCIAL(E) PRODUITS DE LA MER
description:
Selon les directives et consignes de la direction, dans le respect de la politique point de vente, des procédures et des règles d’hygiène/ qualité/ sécurité :

Transformation :

  • Effectue les transformations de produits sur demande des clients (étêtage, éviscération, filetage…).

  • Assure le conditionnement des produits frais emballés marée (mise en barquette, sous vide…).

Vente & relation client :

  • Adopte une attitude commerciale adéquate envers les clients (ex : politesse, courtoisie…).

  • Accueille, conseille, sert et oriente le client en surveillant la rotation et la qualité des produits. Répond aux demandes spécifiques des clients.

  • Participe à l'animation du PDV dans le cadre d'opérations spécifiques.

Gestion / Commandes et livraison :

  • Participe à la mise en place et au suivi des plans d’actions pour rectifier les écarts de chiffre d’affaires, de marge, de quota et de démarque.

  • Suit le niveau de stock, l’historique des opérations (saisonnières et spécifiques).

  • Effectue le relevé des ruptures et prend connaissance des anomalies de stock. En réfère à sa hiérarchie.

  • Prépare et propose les commandes (permanent et promotionnel).

  • Vérifie les livraisons (contrôle qualitatif et quantitatif, présence des mentions obligatoires). Identifie et reporte les litiges fournisseurs à son/sa responsable (établit les bons de non-conformité)

  • Participe à la réalisation des inventaires et à la lutte anti-démarque.

Dynamique commerciale :

  • Est chargé(e) de la mise en place et du suivi des implantations, de la présentation marchande et de la mise en scène des produits, en assure le suivi.

  • Assure la qualité du remplissage des rayons, la présence suffisante de glace, la rotation de produits entre la réserve, la chambre positive et le rayon. Assure leur rangement et leur propreté.

  • Respecte l’assortiment en fonction de la saisonnalité.

  • Est chargé(e) de la mise en place des étiquettes prix, de l’information adéquate (fiches produits, origine, calibre ...), de l’affichage PLV (publicité sur le lieu de vente) et ILV (information sur le lieu de vente), en assure le suivi.

Dynamique commerciale :

  • Effectue le tarage des balances, s’assure de la présence des consommables et de la cohérence des prix entre balances et affiches prix.

  • Réalise les relevés prix concurrents.

  • Distribue le travail entre plusieurs collaborateurs et informe la direction de toutes difficultés de mise en œuvre.

Hygiène/ Qualité/ Sécurité :

  • Respecte les règles d’hygiène et qualité. (ex : effectue le retrait des produits concernés notamment par les dates limites de consommation (D.L.C) et les dates de durabilité minimale (D.D.M) …)

  • Respecte la réglementation en matière d’affichage, d’étiquetage, d’informations clients.

  • Respecte les règles de sécurité (ex : utilisation correcte des outils et du matériel à sa disposition…)

  • Doit porter sa tenue de travail, son badge. Doit veiller à la propreté de sa tenue et à son hygiène personnelle.

Nous recherchons des professionnels dotés d'une résistance physique pour travailler dans un environnement froid et humide.

La précision et la minutie sont indispensables pour la préparation des produits. Le sens du service client s'avère essentiel pour conseiller la clientèle sur les choix et les modes de préparation.

L'autonomie et la rigueur caractérisent les bons professionnels. Le métier demande une capacité d'adaptation aux horaires matinaux et au travail les week-ends. La connaissance approfondie des produits de la mer et la maîtrise des techniques de conservation représentent des atouts majeurs.

title: Associate Sales Professional – North Western Canada
description:
Are you an experienced sales professional who loves the thrill of the build but is tired of the corporate
ladder game? Do you find fulfillment in mastering a specific territory, building genuine long-term
relationships, and being the go-to expert your clients trust year after year? We are specifically seeking a career-minded professional who wants to plant deep roots, own their desk, and enjoy long-term stability in a career in Inside Sales. If you excel at building lasting B2B relationships from the comfort of an office environment and want a role where your tenure and regional expertise are truly valued, this is your destination.
Manitoulin Group of Companies is one of Canada largest national transportation companies, providing
LTL/FTL, dedicated, and logistics solutions throughout North America. We currently have an exceptional
opportunity for anyone looking to establish a career in sales.

Operating out of Acheson (Edmonton)—the gateway to the North—you will focus heavily on our
Northern Alberta, Northern British Columbia, and extended Northern Canadian territories. Success in this
role requires more than just sales acumen; it requires a genuine understanding of the unique geographical,
seasonal, and logistical realities that businesses in Canada’s North face every single day.
Primary Responsibilities
 Territory Ownership: Manage, nurture, and grow our established account base across Northern
Alberta, Northern BC, and the Northern territories.
 New Business Development: Source and secure new customers through targeted cold calls,
competitive research, and strategic outreach.
 Consultative Solutions: Introduce Manitoulin’s extensive suite of services (LTL/FTL,
temperature-controlled, transborder, and dangerous goods) to solve complex remote logistics
challenges.
 Team Collaboration: Support the regional sales team and collaborate closely with operations to
ensure seamless execution for demanding northern routes.
 Client Retention: Act as the steady, reliable, long-term point of contact that our Northern clients
can depend on year after year. (Occasional travel or in-person sales meetings may be required).

 The Northern Advantage: Prior experience or deep knowledge of Canada’s Northern geography
and/or the Transportation/Logistics industry is preferred, particularly regarding Northern
Canadian freight movements and logistics constraints
 Career-Oriented Mindset: You view Inside Sales as a long-term profession. You value
autonomy, consistency, and the opportunity to master a territory.
 Core Skills: You are results-oriented, self-motivated, and a strong communicator with excellent
negotiation, interpersonal, and organizational skills.
 Education: A college or university graduate, or equivalent industry experience.

To Apply
If you are looking to bring your logistics insight to a role where you can truly settle in and make a long-
term impact, we want to hear from you.
For confidential consideration, please send your resume to:
Leanna Di Veronica
Regional Vice President of Sales, West
ldiveronica@manitoulintransport.com
We thank all applicants, but wish to advise that only those under further consideration will be contacted.

What do we offer?

 Stability & Tenure: A permanent, long-term career home with a leading national carrier that
values loyalty.
 Profit Sharing: Direct reward for the company's collective success.
 Competitive Salary: A reliable, strong compensation package.
 Comprehensive Benefits: Full health, dental, and wellness coverage.
 Industry-Leading Infrastructure: The backing of 85+ Canadian terminals, advanced 24/7
shipping technology, and an unparalleled single-source network.

title: (IND) SENIOR, SOFTWARE ENGINEER
location: IN KA BANGALORE Home Office PTPP1
description:
Position Summary...

Walmart+ Membership’s mission is to build a best-in-class retail membership program that delivers unparalleled value, convenience, and savings to our customers. As one of Walmart’s strategic growth pillars, Walmart+ integrates benefits across delivery, fuel, streaming services etc to deepen customer engagement and drive long-term loyalty. Our team is focused on continuously evolving the membership experience through innovation, personalization, and seamless omnichannel integration.

As a Senior Software Engineer on the W+ Membership team, you will have the opportunity to work on high-impact initiatives that shape the future of customer loyalty at Walmart. You will lead the development of scalable platforms that power membership acquisition, retention, and engagement, while ensuring engineering excellence. As an experienced engineer, you will play a key role in defining technical strategy, driving cross-functional collaboration, and delivering customer-centric solutions. This is a high-visibility role with significant opportunities for growth and impact.

What you'll do...

About the team

Walmart+ Membership’s mission is to build a best-in-class retail membership program that delivers unparalleled value, convenience, and savings to our customers. As one of Walmart’s strategic growth pillars, Walmart+ integrates benefits across delivery, fuel, streaming services etc to deepen customer engagement and drive long-term loyalty. Our team is focused on continuously evolving the membership experience through innovation, personalization, and seamless omnichannel integration.

As a Senior Software Engineer on the W+ Membership team, you will have the opportunity to work on high-impact initiatives that shape the future of customer loyalty at Walmart. You will lead the development of scalable platforms that power membership acquisition, retention, and engagement, while ensuring engineering excellence. As an experienced engineer, you will play a key role in defining technical strategy, driving cross-functional collaboration, and delivering customer-centric solutions. This is a high-visibility role with significant opportunities for growth and impact.

What you'll do:

  • Responsible for the design, architecture, and end-to-end delivery of applications/feature , reusable libraries, and frameworks, with a strong emphasis on leveraging AI-powered tools for code generation, automation, and cross-platform development.
  • Raise the bar on best practices, code quality, documentation, testing, monitoring, and feature delivery by actively integrating AI-assisted coding, code review, and testing workflows into the engineering process.
  • Demonstrate strong communication skills to collaborate with stakeholders and cross-functional teams, including sharing AI-driven insights and recommendations for product and process improvements.
  • Champion sustainable engineering by continuously improving best practices, producing best-in-class code, documentation, testing, and monitoring, and adopting AI tools for productivity and quality assurance.
  • Estimate effort, identify risks (including those related to AI adoption), help with prioritization, and devise/meet project schedules.
  • Collaborate with other engineers, providing technical guidance on AI-powered development workflows, code generation, and cross-platform strategies.
  • Rise above details to spot broader issues, trends, and implications for the product/team, including the impact of AI and automation on engineering practices and team productivity

What you'll bring

  • BE/MS/PhD in Computer Science or equivalent.
  • 6-9 years of strong design/development experience overall, with 7+ years of mobile development experience
  • Mobile-first design and responsive code deeply ingrained in your thought process.
  • Expertise in designing and architecting mobile apps.
  • Expertise in iPhone development, including implementing applications with standard iPhone/ iPad UI components, creating custom UI.
  • Expert in Objective C programming & Swift 5 and above, iPhone SDK, SQLite Database, iPad, X Code, XML-JSON Parsing, Location services, Social networking integration like Facebook ,twitter
  • Good knowledge of Core Foundation, Core Data, SQLite
  • Should have good knowledge of Objective-C, Cocoa Touch, iPhone/iPad Apple SDK.
  • Familiarity working with REST APIs for deep integrations with both platforms i.e. general best practices, shortcomings, trade-offs, etc
  • Experience with offline storage, threading, and performance tuning
  • Familiarity with cloud message APIs and push notifications
  • Understanding of Apple’s design principles and interface guidelines
  • Excellent organization, communication, interpersonal and leadership skills.

About Walmart Global Tech
Imagine working in an environment where one line of code can make life easier for hundreds of millions of people. That’s what we do at Walmart Global Tech. We’re a team of software engineers, data scientists, cybersecurity expert's and service professionals within the world’s leading retailer who make an epic impact and are at the forefront of the next retail disruption. People are why we innovate, and people power our innovations. We are people-led and tech-empowered.

We train our team in the skillsets of the future and bring in experts like you to help us grow. We have roles for those chasing their first opportunity as well as those looking for the opportunity that will define their career. Here, you can kickstart a great career in tech, gain new skills and experience for virtually every industry, or leverage your expertise to innovate at scale, impact millions and reimagine the future of retail.

Work
Walmart’s culture sets us apart, and we know being together helps us innovate, learn and grow great careers. This role is based in our [Bangalore/Chennai] office for daily work, with the flexibility for associates to manage their personal lives.

Benefits

Beyond our great compensation package, you can receive incentive awards for your performance. Other great perks include a host of best-in-class benefits maternity and parental leave, PTO, health benefits, and much more.

Belonging

We aim to create a culture where every associate feels valued for who they are, rooted in respect for the individual. Our goal is to foster a sense of belonging, to create opportunities for all our associates, customers and suppliers, and to be a Walmart for everyone.

At Walmart, our vision is "everyone included." By fostering a workplace culture where everyone is—and feels—included, everyone wins. Our associates and customers reflect the makeup of all 19 countries where we operate. By making Walmart a welcoming place where all people feel like they belong, we’re able to engage associates, strengthen our business, improve our ability to serve customers, and support the communities where we operate.

Equal Opportunity Employer

Walmart, Inc., is an Equal Opportunities Employer – By Choice. We believe we are best equipped to help our associates, customers and the communities we serve live better when we really know them. That means understanding, respecting and valuing unique styles, experiences, identities, ideas and opinions – while being inclusive of all people.

Minimum Qualifications...

Outlined below are the required minimum qualifications for this position. If none are listed, there are no minimum qualifications.

Option 1: Bachelor's degree in computer science, computer engineering, computer information systems, software engineering, or related area and 3 years’ experience in software engineering or related area.
Option 2: 5 years’ experience in software engineering or related area.

Preferred Qualifications...

Outlined below are the optional preferred qualifications for this position. If none are listed, there are no preferred qualifications.

Master’s degree in computer science, information technology, engineering, information systems, cybersecurity, or related area and 1 year’s experience leading information security or cybersecurity projects, We value candidates with a background in creating inclusive digital experiences, demonstrating knowledge in implementing Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG) 2.2 AA standards, assistive technologies, and integrating digital accessibility seamlessly. The ideal candidate would have knowledge of accessibility best practices and join us as we continue to create accessible products and services following Walmart’s accessibility standards and guidelines for supporting an inclusive culture.

Information Technology - CISCO Certification - Certification

title: General Merchandise Associate
description:
Position Summary...

Working as a General Merchandise Associate at Walmart Canada is a great way to develop your skills in the retail industry. A General Merchandise Associate provides exemplary customer service by adhering to the basic beliefs and values of Walmart, maintains and zones the general merchandise area, and assists with stocking and the sale of goods. If you are looking for an exciting job in customer service or retail, working as a General Merchandise Associate may be a great fit for you!

What you'll do...

1. Provides excellent Customer Service by greeting all customers in a polite and friendly manner, assists customers by promoting products/services, locating merchandise and making purchase decisions, resolving customer issues and referring concerns where appropriate.
2. Covers an assigned area(s) on the salesfloor.
3. Zones assigned area.
4. Moves feature merchandise to its modular home.
5. Works recovery merchandise from the Customer Service desk and other areas of the store.
6. Assists with Front End coverage.
7. Communicates with other associates to ensure floor coverage is maintained.
8. Contributes to a safe, clean and hazard free work environment through adherence to Company policy and procedures, including the completion of safety sweep logs and ensuring displays and fixtures are secure.
9. Stocks merchandise/picks, Downstocks if applicable . Applies product protection when required. Sets and completes modulars, including flex modulars and sets new shelf caps.

The minimum rate of pay for this position starts from $17.75.

title: On-Call Turndown Attendant
description:
Mandarin Oriental, Boston is looking for an On-Call Turndown Attendant to join our Housekeeping team.

Are you a master of craft? Do you thrive in a team that succeeds together, demonstrating integrity and respect while acting responsibly? Do you embrace a growth mindset? We invite you to become a fan of the exceptional.

Mandarin Oriental is the award-winning owner and operator of some of the most luxurious hotels, resorts and residences located in prime destinations around the world, with a strong development pipeline. Increasingly recognised for creating some of the world’s most sought-after properties, the Group provides legendary service inspired by Asian heritage whilst representing the very cutting-edge of luxury experiences.

Mandarin Oriental, Boston is situated in the heart of the Back Bay overlooking Boylston Street. Our luxurious 138 guest rooms and 12 suites set the standard for spaciousness, combining classic Bostonian residential elegance with refined hints of our Oriental heritage. Our spa introduces Boston to world-acclaimed and unique Mandarin Oriental therapies.

About the job

Based at the Mandarin Oriental, Boston within the Housekeeping Department in Boston, Massachusetts, the Turndown Attendant is responsible for the overall cleanliness or all guestrooms that they are assigned to clean.

The Turndown Attendant reports to the Executive Housekeeper.

As On-Call Turndown Attendant, you will be responsible for the following duties:

  • Place the finishing touches on our hotel rooms for a comfortable and restful evening.
  • Turn down beds, draw curtains, and refresh towels, ensuring everything meets our high housekeeping standards of cleanliness and presentation.
  • Provide warm, professional service to guests, whether addressing their questions or handling special housekeeping requests.
  • Other duties as assigned.

As a Turndown Attendant you will work evening shifts.

As On-Call Turndown Attendant, we expect from you:

  • Possess 1-2 years of experience in the Housekeeping department of a luxury hotel or residential property
  • Reading, writing, and oral proficiency in the English language
  • Strong interpersonal communication skills
  • Ability to work a flexible on-call schedule to include weekends and holidays.

Compensation

The hourly rate of pay for this position is $32.95.

Our commitment to you

  • Learning & Development. Your success is our success. We craft unique learning and development programmes for various stages in your career so that you grow, continuously.
  • MOstay. When you work as hard as our colleagues do, it’s important to take time off. As a member of the #MOfamily, you can stay with us wherever you go in the world. The MOstay programme offers complimentary nights and additionally attractive rates on rooms for you and your loved ones.
  • Heath & Colleague Wellness. Finding the right work-life balance is important. Your wellbeing matters to us. A variety of health benefits and wellness programmes are offered to all our colleagues, globally.
  • Retirement Plans. When you show commitment to us, we reciprocate. We offer different retirement plans depending on the length of your service and your role.

Local Benefits

  • A competitive salary and benefits package
  • Meals served while on duty
  • Discounted colleague parking available
  • Complimentary uniform laundering

Equal Employer Opportunity Statement

Mandarin Oriental Hotel Group provides equal employment opportunities (EEO) to all colleagues and applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability or genetics. In addition to federal law requirements, Mandarin Oriental Hotel Group complies with applicable state and local laws governing non-discrimination in employment in every location in which the company has facilities. This policy applies to all terms and conditions of employment, including recruiting, hiring, placement, promotion, termination, layoff, recall, and transfer, leaves of absence, compensation and training.

Mandarin Oriental Hotel Group expressly prohibits any form of workplace harassment based on race, color, religion, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, national origin, age, genetic information, disability, or veteran status. Improper interference with the ability of Mandarin Oriental Hotel Group’s colleagues to perform their job duties may result in discipline up to and including discharge.

We’re Fans. Are you?

title: Assistant Manager, Material Operations
description:
The Assistant Manager is responsible for the management of all day-to-day operations of the Material Operations department, in conjunction with the department Manager. Through staff and workflow management, the Assistant Manager ensures repair orders are issued parts both systematically and physically. They are also responsible for oversight of repair estimate price and timing and the “direct from customer” repair process. The Assistant Manager provides management support, direction, and development for staff. In addition, the Assistant Manager ensures all schedules are properly updated to ensure adequate coverage and assists with implementation and monitoring of new processes and projects.

Key Accountabilities

  • Quality and Service and Excellence

  • Set goals that align with our luxury brand and meet customer expectations

  • Measure results through daily order reporting, production reporting, and quality monitoring

  • Identify obstacles/opportunities that hinder the daily success of the team

  • Hold the team and yourself accountable to consistently produce high quality work

  • Implement and monitor process improvements

  • Foster a healthy and safe workplace through promoting internal Wellness and Safety programs.

Talent Management

  • Provide leadership to your team by setting clear expectations and holding them accountable

  • Commitment to hiring top talent that can grow one or two levels

  • Develop our current talent by closing skill gaps in order to maximize staff potential through projects and stretch assignments

  • Effectively and appropriately manage employee performance

  • Collaborate with your Manager to coach and develop staff; effectively delegate work

  • Crosstrain our department staff and facility wide in order to create a more flexible work environment

Fiscal Responsibility

  • Plan and forecast for your department taking into consideration historical trends

  • Financial Acumen: Understand your overall business in order to provide strategies for your department; partner with the financial and operations team as appropriate

  • Compliance: Understand industry, corporate, and department regulations in order to manage the team appropriately

  • Workflow management: ensure all work is facilitated through the functions as applicable, managing aged and past due orders appropriately

  • ROI Analysis: Understanding the cost/benefit analysis of your actions

  • Contingency Planning: Being mindful that the business is constantly changing and your role is to provide contingency plans in order to still attain your financial goals

Required Qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s degree

  • 3 years business experience with management background

  • Strong initiative and the ability to work without constant direction

  • Demonstrated ability to identify and provide resolution for areas of improvement

  • Excellent organizational abilities to manage and prioritize multiple tasks

  • Strong academic and/or business background with English language usage, grammatical and communication skills

  • Flexibility with schedule, assignments and willingness to work overtime (possible weekends required during holiday season)

  • High ability to collaborate, build strong business relations and influence without direct

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Proficiency in Tiffany & Co. computer applications (i.e. AS400, MIPS)

  • Experience working in an operations, technical, or luxury retail environment

  • Some familiarity with jewelry, watches, clocks and silver products

  • Experience in direct customer interaction

  • Analytical experience preferred

title: Communication Apprentice
description:
Rejoignez notre équipe Communication pour une expérience formatrice et variée, au cœur des enjeux de communication externe, relations presse et création de contenus, dans un environnement innovant et tourné vers les nouvelles technologies



Communication externe :

Relations presse :

Contenus :
