title: Pluto — Founding AI Infrastructure Engineer
location: New York, New York, United States
description:
Pluto — Founding AI Infrastructure Engineer

Type: Full-time | On-site | New York City, NY Compensation: $250,000–$300,000 + 0.50%–1.00% equity Hiring count: 1 Visa sponsorship: None available — US citizen or permanent resident only Reports to: Alexander, CEO & Co-Founder (no LinkedIn provided)

About Pluto

Pluto is building a CFTC-regulated derivatives exchange to trade GPU compute, positioning itself on the same regulatory footing as the CME and other licensed futures exchanges. It buys GPU hours from neoclouds and resells them to startups in its network, while building the software infrastructure to make compute procurement programmable and liquid. Six-person team, YC Winter 2025, with CFTC futures exchange licensing in process.

Founded: 2024 | Team size: 6 | Total funding: N/A (Seed, YC W2025) Industry: FinTech Website: https://pluto.trade Office: New York City, NY

Why Candidates Should Join

  • Found the technical backbone: No existing playbook — this person defines how Pluto's entire compute infrastructure is built.
  • Genuinely new market: A CFTC-regulated futures exchange for GPU compute, on CME-tier regulatory footing — a category that doesn't exist yet.
  • Strong founding team: Works alongside engineers with backgrounds from Meta, NVIDIA, and AWS; reports directly to the founders.
  • Early equity, early stage: YC W2025, seed stage, 0.50%–1.00% equity.

Intake Call Summary

Not available in source. An intake video is present on the role page, but no transcript or written summary was included in the copied HTML.

The Role

A founding engineering role at the intersection of GPU infrastructure and financial market technology. The engineer owns the technical backbone of Pluto's compute business — the abstraction layer that homogenizes GPU capacity from multiple providers into a unified interface, and the control-plane systems that let customers provision, manage, and pay for clusters through Pluto.

What You'll Be Doing

  • Design and build the customer-facing compute API for provisioning and managing GPU clusters across multiple neocloud providers
  • Build the abstraction layer that normalizes fragmented GPU capacity from different providers into a unified Pluto resource model
  • Own control-plane systems for cluster orchestration, lifecycle management, and state tracking
  • Integrate with GPU providers and neoclouds through APIs, Kubernetes, Slurm, and bare-metal and semi-automated workflows
  • Build internal tooling to replace manual brokerage workflows with repeatable, scalable software systems
  • Develop metering and usage-tracking infrastructure for GPU-hours and customer billing

Tech stack: Python, Go, Rust, or TypeScript; Kubernetes, Slurm, GPU provisioning workflows; APIs and distributed systems

Requirements

  • 3+ years GPU infrastructure or platform engineering experience
  • Low-level GPU familiarity: kernels, Slurm, Kubernetes
  • Neocloud or HFT background strongly preferred
  • US citizen or permanent resident only
  • NYC in-person, startup hours (995)

Green Flags

  • Neocloud or GPU infrastructure background
  • HFT firm experience
  • Strong tenure and deliberate career progression
  • Big tech background with production ownership

Red Flags

  • Excessive job hopping
  • No hands-on GPU or low-level infrastructure experience
  • Not authorized to work in the US

Role Details

  • Salary — $250,000–$300,000
  • Equity — 0.50%–1.00%
  • On-site policy — NYC in-person, startup hours Mon–Fri 9am–9pm (995)
  • Visa sponsorship — None available; US citizen or permanent resident only
  • Employment type — Full-time
  • Location — New York City, NY

Screening Questions

Not provided in source.

Interview Process

Stage 1 — Pending Approval — Candidates awaiting initial approval. Stage 2 — Intro call with Alexander (CEO) — Initial conversation to assess fit and walk through the role. Stage 3 — Technical interview with CTO — First technical deep-dive with Zhang, lead backend engineer and CTO. Stage 4 — Second technical interview with CTO — Second technical round with the CTO. Stage 5 — In-person team dinner or event — Vibe check with the full team in New York. Stage 6 — Offer Extended Stage 7 — Candidate Hired — Candidate accepts and starts.

Ideal Companies & Backgrounds

No formal Ideal Companies section was provided in source. Named signal companies pulled from the role body (for sourcing reference only): Neoclouds — CoreWeave, Lambda HFT firms — SIG, Optiver Big tech — Meta, NVIDIA, AWS

title: Lead, Benefits
description:
About the Company

Gemini is a global crypto and Web3 platform founded by Cameron and Tyler Winklevoss in 2014, offering a wide range of simple, reliable, and secure crypto products and services to individuals and institutions in over 70 countries. Our mission is to unlock the next era of financial, creative, and personal freedom by providing trusted access to the decentralized future. We envision a world where crypto reshapes the global financial system, internet, and money to create greater choice, independence, and opportunity for all — bridging traditional finance with the emerging cryptoeconomy in a way that is more open, fair, and secure. As a publicly traded company, Gemini is poised to accelerate this vision with greater scale, reach, and impact.

The Department: People

The Role: Lead, Benefits

Reporting to the Associate Director, Total Rewards & Payroll, the Lead, Employee Benefits will own Gemini’s employee benefits and leave administration functions across the United States and Singapore. This role is responsible for the strategy, administration, compliance, vendor management, communication, and continuous improvement of Gemini’s employee benefits and leave programs while delivering a high-quality employee experience.

As the subject matter expert for Benefits and Leave Administration, you will have end-to-end accountability for health and welfare programs, employee leaves, vendor partnerships, compliance, reporting, and employee education. You will partner closely with Payroll, Finance, Legal, People Business Partners, Talent Acquisition, managers, and external vendors to ensure our programs remain competitive, compliant, and operationally effective.

This role requires exceptional judgment, discretion, analytical thinking, and the ability to independently drive strategic initiatives while maintaining strong day-to-day execution in a fast-paced environment.

Responsibilities:

  • Own Gemini’s employee benefits and leave administration functions across the United States and Singapore.
  • Lead the strategy, administration, communication, and continuous improvement of all employee benefits and leave programs.
  • Evaluate market trends, employee feedback, utilization data, and business needs to recommend competitive and innovative benefits offerings.
  • Lead benefits renewals in the United States and Singapore, including vendor evaluations, negotiations, plan design recommendations, implementation, and ongoing program management.
  • Own the annual U.S. Open Enrollment process, including planning, system configuration, testing, employee communications, enrollment support, and post-enrollment reconciliation.
  • Manage employee leave programs in partnership with third-party vendors, including FMLA, disability, parental leave, statutory leaves, company-sponsored leaves, and other applicable leave programs.
  • Serve as the primary point of contact for leave administration vendors, ensuring timely case management, accurate employee communications, regulatory compliance, and a consistent employee experience throughout the leave lifecycle.
  • Partner with People Business Partners, Payroll, Legal, managers, and external vendors to coordinate complex leave and accommodation cases.
  • Maintain appropriate confidentiality while ensuring stakeholders receive the information necessary to effectively support employees and manage business operations.
  • Serve as the primary relationship manager for benefits brokers, carriers, leave administrators, consultants, and other external vendors, holding partners accountable to service levels, contractual commitments, and compliance requirements.
  • Partner with Payroll, Finance, Legal, and People Operations to ensure accurate benefits administration, payroll deductions, funding, reporting, and regulatory compliance.
  • Develop and execute employee communications and education initiatives that improve awareness, understanding, and utilization of Gemini’s benefits and leave programs.
  • Partner with Talent Acquisition to ensure employee benefits are accurately and effectively represented throughout the candidate experience.
  • Build and maintain benefits and leave reporting, dashboards, and analytics to measure utilization, cost, vendor performance, employee engagement, and program effectiveness.
  • Monitor leave trends, case management outcomes, vendor performance, and employee feedback, and recommend improvements to strengthen the employee experience and operational efficiency.
  • Standardize, document, and continuously improve benefits and leave processes, controls, policies, and procedures across supported jurisdictions.
  • Ensure compliance with all applicable federal, state, and local requirements within the United States, as well as applicable benefits and leave requirements in Singapore.
  • Act as the escalation point for complex and sensitive employee benefits and leave questions.
  • Support internal and external audits, regulatory reporting, testing, and compliance activities related to benefits and employee leaves.
  • Identify opportunities to automate processes, strengthen controls, reduce administrative burden, and improve service delivery.
  • Partner with the broader Total Rewards team on strategic initiatives where benefits or leave expertise is required.

Minimum Qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Human Resources, Business Administration, or a related field, or an equivalent combination of relevant education, training, and experience.
  • 8+ years of progressive experience administering and managing employee benefits programs.
  • Demonstrated experience owning a corporate benefits function with end-to-end accountability.
  • Experience supporting benefits programs in the United States and international markets; experience supporting Singapore is strongly preferred.
  • Demonstrated experience leading U.S. benefits renewals and annual Open Enrollment.
  • Experience administering employee leave programs in partnership with third-party vendors, including FMLA, disability, parental leave, and other statutory or company-sponsored leave programs.
  • Working knowledge of U.S. employee benefits and leave requirements, including FMLA, ADA, and applicable state and local leave laws.
  • Experience managing benefits brokers, carriers, leave administrators, consultants, and third-party vendors.
  • Strong analytical, problem-solving, and critical-thinking skills, with the ability to make data-informed recommendations.
  • Excellent project management and organizational skills, with the ability to independently manage multiple priorities and deadlines.
  • Strong client-service orientation and the ability to manage complex or sensitive employee matters with discretion and empathy.
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills, including the ability to explain complex benefits and leave information clearly.
  • Demonstrated judgment and ability to manage highly confidential and sensitive information.
  • Strong attention to detail and commitment to operational accuracy.
  • Ability to thrive in a fast-paced and evolving environment while balancing strategic initiatives with day-to-day execution.

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Experience administering benefits through Dayforce and/or Workday, including benefits configuration and Open Enrollment.
  • Experience supporting a transition from Workday to Dayforce or another HRIS or payroll platform.
  • Experience administering employee benefits and leave programs in Singapore.
  • Experience managing third-party leave administration vendors and supporting complex leave and accommodation cases.
  • Experience supporting benefits implementations, HRIS migrations, or system optimization initiatives.
  • Experience developing benefits and leave communications, employee education programs, and manager resources.
  • Demonstrated ability to use analytics to evaluate and improve benefits and leave program effectiveness.
  • Proven ability to identify opportunities for automation, process improvement, stronger controls, and improved employee service.
  • Demonstrated ability to manage immediate operational priorities while advancing longer-term strategic initiatives.
It Pays to Work Here
The compensation & benefits package for this role includes:
  • Competitive starting pay
  • A discretionary annual bonus
  • Long-term incentive in the form of a new hire equity grant
  • Comprehensive health plans
  • 401K with company matching
  • Paid Parental Leave
  • Flexible time off

Salary Range: The base salary range for this role is between $112,000 - $160,000 in the State of New York. This range is not inclusive of our discretionary bonus or equity package. When determining a candidate’s compensation, we consider a number of factors including skillset, experience, job scope, and current market data.

In the United States, we offer a hybrid work approach at our hub offices, balancing the benefits of in-person collaboration with the flexibility of remote work. Expectations may vary by location and role, so candidates are encouraged to connect with their recruiter to learn more about the specific policy for the role. Employees who do not live near one of our hubs are part of our remote workforce.

At Gemini, we strive to build diverse teams that reflect the people we want to empower through our products, and we are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity, or Veteran status. Equal Opportunity is the Law, and Gemini is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace. If you have a specific need that requires accommodation, please let a member of the People Team know.

title: Architekt IT
description:
Cześć!

Z tej strony Neontri - jesteśmy firmą liczącą ponad 120 osób. Specjalizujemy się w dwóch obszarach:

-tworzymy systemy, które zlecają nam nasi Klienci (najczęściej tworzymy coś od zera, albo dodajemy w pełni nowe funkcjonalności) głównie z obszaru: finansowego, bankowego, fin-tech, e-commerce - choć nie tylko :)

-wspieramy naszych Klientów w rekrutacji Specjalistów, a naszym Kandydatom staramy się dobrać jak najlepiej dopasowane oferty (tzw. bodyleasing)

Co może nas wyróżniać spośród innych firm i dlaczego warto rozpocząć z nami współpracę?

  1. Posiadamy bogate doświadczenie w realizacji dużych, złożonych projektów. Miliony użytkowników codziennie korzystają z aplikacji, które współtworzymy.
  2. Większość projektów jest prowadzona długoterminowo i w sposób zdalny. Dbamy o nasze relacje i możliwość cyklicznych spotkań na integracjach. Staramy się także angażować w akcje charytatywne.
  3. W codziennej współpracy stawiamy nacisk na szczerość, zaufanie i dobre relacje. Projekty realizowane są w mniejszych zespołach ze zróżnicowanym seniority.

title: Responsable Hébergement
description:
MANDARIN ORIENTAL PARIS is looking for a Room Division Manager to join our room division Team.

Are you a master of craft? Do you thrive in a team that succeeds together, demonstrating integrity and respect while acting responsibly? Do you embrace a growth mindset? We invite you to become a fan of the exceptional.

Mandarin Oriental Hotel Group is the award-winning owner and operator of some of the most luxurious hotels, resorts and residences located in prime destinations around the world, with a strong development pipeline. Increasingly recognised for creating some of the world’s most sought-after properties, the Group provides legendary service inspired by Asian heritage whilst representing the very cutting-edge of luxury experiences.

Mandarin Oriental Paris embodies the spirit of the Paris of today and tomorrow. This oasis of tranquillity vibrates to the rhythm of fashion and artistic creation, sublimated by the legendary service of the Mandarin Oriental group. The hotel boasts 135 rooms, Restaurant Le Camélia, Bar 8 and one of the most beautiful and extensive Spas in Paris.

About the job

The Room Division Manager is responsible for the overall leadership, performance, and strategic direction of the Rooms Division, including Front Office, Guest Experience, Concierge, Housekeeping. The role ensures exceptional guest satisfaction, operational excellence, revenue optimization, talent development, and financial performance while maintaining Mandarin Oriental's Legendary Quality Experiences (LQE) and service standards.

The Rooms Division Manager serves as a key member of the hotel's leadership team, driving collaboration across departments and contributing to the achievement of hotel business objectives.

As Room Division Manager, you will be responsible for the following duties:

  • Lead and develop the Rooms Division team through recruitment, training, coaching, and performance management.
  • Foster a culture of excellence based on accountability, collaboration, inclusion, and Mandarin Oriental values.
  • Ensure an exceptional guest experience throughout the entire guest journey, from arrival to departure.
  • Drive service quality by implementing LQE standards, monitoring guest feedback, and leading continuous improvement initiatives.
  • Oversee all Rooms Division operations, including Front Office, Concierge, Housekeeping.
  • Optimize operational efficiency and productivity through effective workforce planning, procedures, service standards, and regulatory compliance.
  • Enhance financial performance by maximizing room revenue through occupancy growth, upselling, and revenue management strategies.
  • Control costs and manage budgets to achieve profitability and operational targets.
  • Monitor and analyse key performance indicators (KPIs) related to financial results, guest satisfaction, team productivity, and operational effectiveness.
  • Contribute to the hotel's overall strategy by participating in executive committee decisions, transformation projects, renovations, process improvements, and risk/compliance management.

As Room Division Manager, we expect from you:

  • Minimum 8–10 years of luxury hotel operations experience.
  • Minimum 3–5 years in a senior Rooms Division leadership position.
  • Strong understanding of Front Office, Housekeeping, Guest Experience, Concierge, and Spa operations.
  • Strategic leadership
  • Financial acumen
  • Revenue optimization
  • Talent development
  • Guest-centric mindset
  • Change management
  • Problem solving and analytical thinking
  • Excellent communication and stakeholder management
  • Operational excellence
  • Fluent French and English.
  • Additional languages preferred.

Our commitment to you

  • Learning & Development. Your success is our success. We craft unique learning and development programmes for various stages in your career so that you grow, continuously.
  • MOstay. When you work as hard as our colleagues do, it’s important to take time off. As a member of the #MOfamily, you can stay with us wherever you go in the world. The MOstay programme offers complimentary nights and additionally attractive rates on rooms for you and your loved ones.
  • Heath & Colleague Wellness. Finding the right work-life balance is important. Your wellbeing matters to us. A variety of health benefits and wellness programmes are offered to all our colleagues, globally.
  • Retirement Plans. When you show commitment to us, we reciprocate. We offer different retirement plans depending on the length of your service and your role.

Local benefits

  • 13th month (from 4 months' seniority in the 1st year)
  • 100% public transport reimbursement
  • Mutual insurance 60% covered

“This job advert is published on behalf of SH Management, owner of Mandarin Oriental, Paris”

title: (CAN) OMNI Customer Fulfillment Associate
description:
Position Summary...

Working as an OMNI Customer Fulfillment Associate at Walmart Canada is a great opportunity to build valuable skills in a fast‑paced retail environment. In this role, you pick, pack, and dispense online orders with a strong focus on accuracy, quality, and customer satisfaction, while following safety and food hygiene standards. By helping deliver a seamless online shopping experience, you play an important role in building customer trust and loyalty. This position is well suited for individuals seeking an engaging role in customer service or retail.

What you'll do...

WHAT YOU'LL DO

Pick, Pack, and Prepare Customer Orders
Picks and assembles customer orders from different temperature areas with care and accuracy to support on-time delivery. Packs orders carefully by confirming product selection, quantities, and product codes.

Ensure Product Quality and Order Accuracy
Selects fresh, high-quality products and removes damaged or unsuitable items. Completes all required documentation and labels accurately, including pick slips and shipping labels.

Maintain Efficient and Organized Packing
Packs totes efficiently to make the best use of space while maintaining product quality and safety standards.

Support Customers and Team Operations
Balances fulfillment tasks with positive customer interaction, providing assistance and helping resolve issues when needed.

Follow Safety, Food Hygiene, and Quality Standards
Maintains a clean and safe work area by following food hygiene and safety procedures, cleaning spills promptly, and ensuring totes are clean before use. Operates equipment safely and completes required safety checks. Demonstrates flexibility and contributes ideas to improve processes, productivity, and customer satisfaction.

Flexibility (Availability/Work Schedule)
• Be open to working a variety of shifts, including evenings, weekends, and holidays, based on business needs. Your adaptability helps ensure the store runs smoothly and customers receive consistent, high-quality service.

POTENTIAL CHALLENGES IN THIS ROLE

High Task Volume in a Fast-Paced Environment
• Manages overnight hours effectively and planning accordingly to stay energized and focused.
• Effectively manages work hours and plans ahead to maintain energy and focus throughout the shift.
• Handles a fast-paced workload with efficiency, ensuring large volumes of restocking and recovery are completed accurately and on time.

Physical Demands
• Performs physical tasks such as standing for extended periods, bending, and lifting inventory, equipment, or supplies weighing up to 20 lbs., and occasionally up to 50 lbs.
• Performs repetitive tasks such as stocking, zoning, and organizing products with attention to detail and consistency to uphold store standards.

WHY YOU'LL ENJOY WORKING HERE

Energetic, Fast-Paced Environment
Thrive in a dynamic setting where every day brings something new.

Appreciation for Your Efforts
Your contributions are recognized and valued.

Career Growth Opportunities
Build your skills and advance your career with us.

Meaningful Impact
Take pride in helping customers save money and live better.

The minimum rate of pay for this position starts from $17.75.

title: Nurse Technician - 2E | Part Time | Days or Nights
description:
At Singing River Health System, caring isn’t just part of the job — it’s in our DNA. Whether we’re saving lives, supporting behind the scenes, or showing up for our community, we bring the energy, heart, and hustle every single day.

With pride, passion, and commitment, we will improve health and save lives in our community every day.

Shift:

Days or Nights

Location:

Singing River Ocean Springs Hospital

Job Description Summary

Job Description

Position Overview:
The Nurse Technician provides fundamentals of nursing care (i.e. personal and restorative care) to patients according to predefined guidelines, a plan of care established by a Registered Nurse, and under the direct supervision of the Registered Nurse staff. Duties for this position will reflect the semester completed within the NLN. Expectation is for all performed duties to be in accordance with Singing River Health System procedures and policies, accreditation organization, and governing guidance and publications for health care employees.

DISCLAIMER: This is not necessarily an exhaustive list of all responsibilities, duties, skills, efforts, requirements or working conditions associated with the job. While this intends to be an accurate reflection of the current job, management reserves the right to revise the job or to require that other or different tasks performed as assigned.

Education:
Must be currently enrolled in an NLN School of Nursing. At completion of each semester Nurse Tech is required to provide new transcript to Human Resources.

Nurse Tech I – enrolled in a nursing program
Nurse Tech II – completed level I, enrolled in level II
Nurse Tech III – completed level II, enrolled in level III
Nurse Tech IV – completed level III, enrolled in level IV

License:
N/A

Certifications:
Must complete and maintain BCLS certification upon hire and/or transfer.
Must have de-escalation training completed by the end of position orientation (90 days); must have appropriate level of de-escalation training.

Experience:
None

Reports to:
RN responsible for patient care management/patient care during a shift, Patient Care Coordinator, Patient Care Manager

Supervises:
None

Physical Demands:
Work is moderately active: involves sitting with frequent requirements to move about the office, move about the facility, and to travel to another facility within the SRHS service area. Work involves exerting a negligible amount of force frequently to lift, carry, push, pull, or otherwise move objects, including the human body.

Work involves using many physical motions in performing daily work activities; subject to exposure of body fluids, sputum and tissues, which may carry the hazard of infectious disease. Work involves using repetitive motions: substantial movements of the wrists, hands, and or fingers while operating standard office equipment such as computer keyboard.

Work involves being able to perceive the nature of sound at normal speaking levels with or without correction; the ability to make fine discriminations in sound. Work requires close visual and acuity and the ability to adjust the eye to bring an object into sharp focus, i.e. shift gaze from viewing a computer monitor to forms/printed material that are closer to compare data at close vision.

Must be able to be active for extended periods of time without experiencing undue fatigue. Must be able to work schedules assigned with the understanding that changes may be instituted according to the needs of the hospital for off days, shifts or weekends.

Mental Demands:
Must demonstrate keen mental faculties/assessment and decision making abilities. Must demonstrate superior communication/speaking/enunciation skills to receive and give information in person and by telephone. Must demonstrate strong written and verbal communication skills. Must possess emotional stability conducive to dealing with high stress levels. Must demonstrate ability to work under pressure and meet deadlines.
Attention to detail and the ability to multi-task in complex situations is required. Must have the ability to maintain collaborative and respectable working relationships throughout SRHS and other organizations.

Special Demands:
Must possess superior customer service skills and professional etiquette. Must possess proficient knowledge and ability to use a computer (must be keyboard proficient) and other office technology (i.e., telephone, fax, etc.), MS Outlook and Word.

Must be able to demonstrate appropriate clinical judgment and apply appropriate professional skills to a patient population of all ages.

If you’re looking for purpose, teamwork, and a place where what you do truly matters, you’ve found it. Let’s do big things together. Apply now and join our team.

title: Senior Growth Marketing Manager | Barcelona
description:
If running paid campaigns across a bunch of platforms is your happy place, and you think as much about the journey as the click, this could be a good fit. You're in Google Ads in the morning, LinkedIn Campaign Manager over lunch, and Reddit or TikTok in the afternoon. In between, you're thinking about how these campaigns work together to move real people through our funnel.

We're hiring a Senior Growth Marketing Manager to plan, deploy, and optimize campaigns across our paid channels. This role isn't just about setting campaigns live and watching the numbers. It's about thinking carefully about the marketing funnel, inventing new ways to bring quality leads and accounts into it, and using paid media to move them all the way through to sales-ready. You'll work closely with our Senior Director of Creative Performance Marketing, turning creative briefs and campaign strategies into live campaigns that actually perform. You'll own the technical craft of paid media (structure, targeting, bidding, testing, iteration) and the strategic craft of designing campaigns that do their job at each funnel stage.

This is an individual contributor role with no direct reports. What you'll be doing is deep, hands-on platform work combined with real strategic thinking about how paid fits into the broader journey.

Day to day, you'll:

• Plan campaign architecture across platforms with clear funnel intent (awareness, consideration, decision) and matching audience, creative, and CTA logic for each stage

• Get creative about lead acquisition itself: figure out how to reach new audiences, test new platforms and formats, and keep growing both the quantity and quality of leads coming into the funnel

• Design retargeting and lifecycle audiences that work with email nurture, content offers, and sales outreach to move leads through the funnel

• Partner with the rest of the marketing team on lead qualification and hand-off criteria, so what reaches sales is actually ready to convert

• Contribute creative direction to ad concepts and copy, working with our creative resources on work that resonates

• Build and launch campaigns in Google Ads, Microsoft Ads, LinkedIn, Meta (Facebook and Instagram), Reddit, TikTok, and emerging platforms like Chat GPT Ads

• Set up and manage audience targeting, including custom audiences, lookalikes, contextual, intent, and account-based lists

• Own bid strategy selection and budget pacing across campaigns

• Traffic creative assets, keep naming conventions consistent, and manage UTMs

• Set up conversion tracking (pixels, server-side, offline conversions) and make sure GA4 is telling us the truth

• Run structured A/B tests on copy, creative, audiences, and bidding

• Pull data, build reports, and share what's working (and what isn't) with the team

• Continuously optimize campaigns, moving budget toward what's performing and shutting off what isn't

What matters most

You should be a technically strong paid media operator with meaningful hands-on time across multiple platforms. Not just Google. Not just Meta. You should be genuinely comfortable in three or more, and hungry to learn new ones as they emerge.

Just as important: you should think in funnels, not just campaigns. The point of running paid isn't to hit CTR benchmarks in isolation. It's to move real people from not knowing us to considering us to choosing us. You'll design campaigns that work alongside nurture, content, and sales to do their actual job at each stage of the journey.

You'll also need to be a creative problem-solver about lead acquisition itself. Not just running the campaigns we've imagined, but figuring out how to reach audiences we haven't reached yet, inventing new ways to bring net new leads and accounts into the funnel, and partnering with the rest of marketing so those leads are qualified for sales when they hand off. Growing both the quantity and quality of leads in the pipeline is the real measure of success.

And the best paid media leads bring real creative instinct alongside the technical craft. They can look at ad copy and know why it will or won't land. They can spot the audience insight that turns a decent campaign into a great one. They have opinions about hooks, headlines, and offers.

That creative sensibility, on top of the technical skills, is what we're really looking for.

  • 5+ years of hands-on paid media experience across multiple platforms

  • Advanced expertise in Google Ads, LinkedIn Campaign Manager, and Meta Ads Manager

  • Working experience with at least two of: Reddit Ads, TikTok Ads Manager, ChatGPT Ads, YouTube, Microsoft Ads, programmatic, or other emerging platforms

  • Strong understanding of the marketing funnel and how paid media integrates with nurture, content, and sales to move leads through it

  • Real creative instinct: you have opinions about ad copy, hooks, and concepts, and can point to work you've influenced or written that performed

  • Creative problem-solving around lead acquisition: a track record of finding new ways to reach audiences and grow both the quantity and quality of leads in the funnel

  • Strong grip on conversion tracking (pixels, server-side, offline conversions, GA4)

  • Experience building custom audiences, lookalikes, and account-based targeting

  • Structured A/B testing experience and a track record of using test results to actually improve performance

  • Comfortable in dashboards and pulling your own data

  • Bachelor's degree

    Nice to have: experience with paid campaigns in multilingual markets. Localization, SaaS, professional services, or complex B2B global marketing experience. Hands-on with AI tools in performance workflows (creative variation, audience research, copy testing, predictive bidding, personalization). Familiarity with attribution modeling beyond last-click. Working knowledge of

    SEO, CRO, or organic social.

    A quick note on fit

    This role sits at the intersection of technical execution, funnel strategy, and creative problem-solving (both on the ad craft side and on the lead acquisition side). If you love the platforms, have real opinions about creative, think about paid as a way to move people through a journey, and enjoy the challenge of finding new ways to bring quality leads in, you'll thrive here. If you're purely a media buyer with no creative interest, or purely a creative with no platform depth, one side of this role will probably feel like a stretch. Applicants should be ready to talk through specific campaigns they've built, the creative behind them, how those campaigns fit into the funnel, and an example or two of creative problem-solving on lead acquisition.

title: Building Materials Analyst
description:
About the Role

The Building Materials Analyst prepares samples and measures specifications using prescribed lab equipment for each product. Analyzes samples by following established procedures in order to determine sample condition and specifications. Records data and measurements. Conducts database searches, identify selections, make report comments, and generates validation reports based on correct specifications. Maintains optimal efficiency and quality standards.

Key Responsibilities

  • Measures, evaluates, and analyzes all sample specifications, database selections, websites, and library samples to ensure accuracy in determining like, kind & quality selections or matches.
  • Operates and utilizes lab equipment/tools as required, following current lab procedures.
  • Documents and verifies all unusual specifications, poor sample conditions, and any aspects of individual samples that does not comply with industry norms by placing internal notes and report comments in ITEL software.
  • Communicates with manufacturers and distributors as required by specific products.
  • Generates a validation report containing findings of the analysis and product recommendations.
  • Files and stores samples according to set procedures.
  • Assists in “Disaster Recovery” implementation.
  • Assists other departments as needed or directed.

Education & Experience

Required:

  • High school diploma or equivalent
  • At least two (2) years of job-related experience preferred in the flooring, siding, or roofing industries
  • Must be detail-oriented and have the ability to evaluate mathematical data and arrive at a decision
  • Ability to operate light industrial machinery, measuring devices (ie. microscopes, calipers, vision systems, spectrometers, etc.), and a variety of general tools
  • Ability to effectively balance multiple tasks
  • Effective problem solving and decision making
  • Proficient with Microsoft Office Suite or related software

Preferred:

  • Some college coursework

Key Competencies

  • Results-Oriented: ability to plan, schedule and organize professional schedule to achieve strategic goals within or ahead of established time frames
  • Adaptability to Change: ability to be flexible and supportive, react swiftly to and able to positively and proactively assimilate change in rapid growth environment
  • Interpersonal Communication: ability to choose a communication behavior that is both appropriate and effective for a given situation; the ability to understand and manage your own emotions, as well as recognize and influence the emotions of others
  • Team Orientation and Collaboration: ability to successfully build and maintain collaborative relationships to work effectively together as a team through shared responsibility, respect, and empathy to complete a shared goal for a common good
  • Accountability: ability to act with a clear sense of ownership; takes personal responsibility for decisions, actions, deliverables, and failures; establishes clear responsibilities and processes for monitoring work and measuring results; embraces experimentation, creativity, and positive change
  • Cultural Competence: ability to understand and respect values, attitudes, beliefs, and mores of the member that differ across cultures, and to consider and respond appropriately to these differences in planning, implementing, and evaluating health programs and interventions

Why you'll love working at Nearmap:

We move fast and work smart; often wearing multiple hats. We’re proud of our inclusive, supportive culture, and maintain a safe environment where everyone feels a sense of belonging and can be themselves.

Learn More About The Work We Do

YouTube Page

LinkedIn Page

title: Simbonis Librarian for User Experience
description:
Working at Yale means contributing to a better tomorrow. Whether you are a current resident of our New Haven-based community, eligible for opportunities through the New Haven Hiring Initiative, or a newcomer, interested in exploring all that Yale has to offer, your talents and contributions are welcome. Discover your opportunities at Yale!

Overview

Schedule: Full-time (37.5 hours per week), hybrid with 4 days required on site

Monday – Friday 8:30am - 5pm, Occasional nights/weekends may be required.

The Simbonis Fellowship is a two-year term appointment from the date of hire. Anticipated start date 9/1/2026. The position is made possible by the Stanley Simbonis Fund.

Reporting to the Head of Access & Delivery Services, the Simbonis Librarian for User Experience serves the Yale Schools of Medicine, Nursing and Public Health and the broader Medical Campus through assessment, outreach, and reference activities. In partnership with a supportive team, the Simbonis Librarian for User Experience, engages with patrons and medical library staff to understand their diverse needs through usability testing and workflow assessment. Through this work, this librarian will leverage user-centered design to review, develop, and improve library processes and workflows to ensure their efficiency and usability while also gathering, analyzing, and communicating data that shows the impact and value of library services, spaces, and resources. As a member of the Access & Delivery Services department, this role is responsible for supporting the daily operations of the department and coordinating outreach activities that support academic success, wellness, and engagement.

Experienced and recent/projected MLS/MLIS graduates are encouraged to apply. You must have your degree completed by or within 90 days of start date. The fellowship allows the selected candidate the unique opportunity to work with several program areas, collaborate on projects, and to participate in a challenging and rewarding work environment. A flexible program will be developed to balance the candidate’s individual goals and interests with the library’s needs and with an eye to developing a few key projects to present at conferences. In addition, the librarian’s professional interests will be supported with a mentoring program, professional development opportunities, and participation on library committees.


Required Skills and Abilities

1. Excellent interpersonal, analytical and communications skills, including the ability to actively listen, understand and translate user needs into functional system improvements.

2. Commitment to providing excellent customer service standards and a passion for user-centered design.

3. Demonstrated ability to prioritize, multi-task and meet deadlines, and to conceptualize and conceive new solutions to problems with creativity and flexibility.

4. Ability to work both independently or collaboratively in a team environment, and to effectively build partnerships and promote the benefits of change in an academic culture that often values ambiguity, diversity of opinion and historic precedent.

5. Ability to design and implement effective programs, projects and services and bring them to fruition or conclusion promptly to achieve library objectives.

Knowledge of techniques for evaluating/assessing information literacy programs and instruction.

Principal Responsibilities

1. Collaborates on projects with colleagues on the Academic Research and Education Team who support students and faculty at the Yale Schools of Medicine Public Health, and Nursing, colleagues from the Clinical Research and Education Team who support the patient care, research and education needs of clinicians at Yale New Haven Hospital, and colleagues from the Technology and Innovation Team who provide data, informatics, web, and technical support across the medical campus. 2. Provides reference, information, citation management, research, and consultation services for students and researchers throughout the research life cycle. 3. Participates in the medical library's general instruction program and prepares and presents a range of classes on various topics, which could include biomedical databases and platforms, open science and other scholarly publication trends, citation management tools, biomedical research strategies, enhancing research impact, and citation analysis. Classes may be held in the library or other campus locations and/or online. 4. Works on projects related to scholarly communication, open access, and publishing. Helps promote new publishing models to diverse user groups. 5. Creates and maintains online guides and other research support tools for emerging areas of interest. 6. Participates in departmental, library, and system-wide planning, and engages in campus, regional and national professional organizations, and collaborative activities. 7. May serve as a project manager for short-term projects. 8. Serves on departmental committees. 9. Demonstrates professional development and service through publication and/or active membership in professional organizations. 10. May be required to assist in disaster recovery efforts. 11. May perform other duties as assigned. Required Education and Experience 1. Master’s degree from an ALA‐accredited library school. In selected instances, a graduate degree in a related discipline may be substituted for a master’s degree in library science. 2. Experience working collegially, collaboratively and independently with varied groups with and across a complex organization, and a rapidly changing team environment.

07/23/2026

Job Category

Professional

Bargaining Unit

(LIB)

Salary Range

$68,000.00 - $145,250.00

Background Check Requirements

All candidates for employment will be subject to pre-employment background screening for this position, which may include motor vehicle, DOT certification, drug testing and credit checks based on the position description and job requirements. All offers are contingent upon the successful completion of the background check. For additional information on the background check requirements and process visit "Learn about background checks" under the Applicant Support Resources section of Careers on the It's Your Yale website.

Health Requirements

Certain positions have associated health requirements based on specific job responsibilities. These may include vaccinations, tests, or examinations, as required by law, regulation, or university policy.

Posting Disclaimer

Salary offers are determined by a candidate’s qualifications, experience, skills, and education in relation to the position requirements, along with the role’s grade profile and current internal and external market conditions.


The intent of this job description is to provide a representative summary of the essential functions that will be required of the position and should not be construed as a declaration of specific duties and responsibilities of the position. Employees will be assigned specific job-related duties through their hiring department.


The University is committed to basing judgments concerning the admission, education, and employment of individuals upon their qualifications and abilities and seeks to attract to its faculty, staff, and student body qualified persons from a broad range of backgrounds and perspectives. In accordance with this policy and as delineated by federal and Connecticut law, Yale does not discriminate in admissions, educational programs, or employment against any individual on account of that individual’s sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, race, color, national or ethnic origin, religion, age, disability, status as a special disabled veteran, veteran of the Vietnam era or other covered veteran.

Inquiries concerning Yale's Policy Against Discrimination and Harassment may be referred to the Office of Institutional Equity and Accessibility (OIEA).

title: HÔTE DE CAISSE (H/F)
description:
Sous la responsabilité de votre manager, vous avez un rôle clé dans la satisfaction de nos clients en étant disponible et bienveillant. En accueillant les clients au moment de leur passage en caisse, vous participez à leur fidélisation. Vous effectuez l’enregistrement des achats et réalisez l’encaissement dans le respect des procédures afin d’assurer le bon déroulement du passage en caisse. Vous êtes responsable de la bonne tenue de votre caisse dans un objectif d’amélioration de l’expérience client.

Chez Intermarché, l’audace et l’envie d’entreprendre sont un véritable état d’esprit. Vous avez envie de vous investir et d’évoluer au sein d’une équipe de passionnés et qui saura reconnaître votre talent ? Dynamique et enthousiaste, vous avez un réel sens du contact client et grâce à vos qualités relationnelles, vous savez créer avec vos clients des liens de confiance et de proximité pour toujours leur apporter conseils et valeur ajoutée.

Vous bénéficiez d’une rémunération attractive composée d’un salaire fixe + 13ème mois + intéressement + mutuelle, 5 semaines de congés payés et 5% de remise sur vos achats.
Nos parcours de développement des compétences vous aideront à évoluer rapidement.
Vivez votre passion et engagez-vous à nos côtés au sein d’une équipe humaine et conviviale !

Seules les compétences font la différence. Le Groupement Mousquetaires s'engage activement en faveur de la diversité, en créant un environnement de travail inclusif qui garantit l'égalité des opportunités pour chacun(e).

title: (USA) Realty Project Coach
description:
Position Summary...

What you'll do...

As a Realty Project Coach, you'll join our Realty Execution team for Walmart US. You will take ownership in delivering store and club remodels, new stores and clubs, and special projects across the US. Your responsibility will be to ensure projects are done on time, within budget, and meet our top-notch quality standards. In this role, you'll partner with store team leads to supervise hourly team members, handle any escalations, and collaborate with store leadership to keep everything on track. You'll have the opportunity to travel to projects across the US. Walmart truly stands out as the best place to build a career from the ground up. No other company can rival our combination of making a massive impact and our culture of promoting from within, from entry-level roles all the way to executive positions. Thanks to our unique mix of career pathways, perks, and pay, you can craft just about any career you dream of here, no matter where you start or what you aspire to achieve. Why You'll Love This Role:
  • Influencing Others: Motivate your team to meet timelines and deliverables on projects. Encourage collaboration and teamwork among associates. Reinforce what teamwork looks like by resolving store leadership, supplier, and associate concerns.
  • Project Ownership: Own the execution of projects in your assigned stores. Communicate plans, changes, and obstacles to key stakeholders. Understand plans and minimize impact on store operations.
  • Develop Associates: Provide supervision and development opportunities for your team members. Spend time listening and acting on ideas, suggestions, questions, or concerns. Evaluate talent, train and mentor, provide recognition, and identify career paths for associates.
Your Resume Will Stand Out With:
  • Demonstrated knowledge of construction remodels, project management, space management, and/or store design.
  • Experience with leading people, projects, initiatives, or leading cross-functional teams.
Shift: Primarily working overnights; night shifts average from 10 to 12 hours. Travel: Frequent travel up to 1000 miles from home is a role requirement. Expect to be away from home 80% of the time, or for 17-21 days at a time. Travel areas are based on project workload and will change as the business needs. Associate will be based out of a home store but can expect to travel to surrounding states or farther to support projects. Mileage will be reimbursed, and overnight stays will be paid for RPC roles. Salary: The annual salary range for this position is $60,000 - $110,000. Minimum Qualifications:

  • Provide and support the implementation of business solutions by building relationships and partnerships with key stakeholders; identifying business needs; determining and carrying out necessary processes and practices; monitoring progress and results; recognizing and capitalizing on improvement opportunities; and adapting to competing demands, organizational changes, and new responsibilities.

title: Test Manager / Test Lead (French Speaker)
description:
• Lead and manage end-to-end testing activities across multiple projects and business domains.

  • Solid knowledge of software testing principles, methodologies, and best practices.
  • ISTQB certification or equivalent testing knowledge is highly valued.
  • Previous experience in testing projects within the Life Insurance, Insurance, or Banking sectors is highly desirable.
  • Experience managing testing activities across multiple projects and stakeholders.

title: Pro Fee Coding Specialist - Trauma Coder
description:
Current Saint Francis Employees - Please click HERE to login and apply.

Full Time

Days

Schedule: Monday through Friday, 8-hour shifts primarily during standard business hours. Candidates must be flexible to adjust their schedule as needed to support departmental and operational needs.

Job Summary: The Pro Fee Coding Specialist reviews documentation and reviews, adds or corrects diagnosis and procedure codes that have been submitted by the provider. This role utilizes coding knowledge learned through valid coding resources in decision making.

Minimum Education: GED or High School diploma.

Licensure, Registration and/or Certification: (CCS) Certified Coding Specialist - American Health Information Management Association (AHIMA), (CPC) Certified Professional Coder - American Association of Professional Coders (AAPC), (BCHH-C) Board Certified Home Health Coding Credentialing – WellSky, (RHIA) Registered Health Information Administrator - American Health Information Management Association (AHIMA), (RHIT) Registered Health Information Technician - American Health Information Management Association (AHIMA), or Hierarchical Conditions Categories (HCCS) from The Compliance Certification Board (CCB). The applicant will need to obtain the certification within one year of hire if they do not have a required certification.

Work Experience: None. Experience and/or training in the anatomy and physiology of the human body and disease processes in order to understand the etiology, pathology, symptoms, signs, diagnostic studies, treatment modalities, and prognosis of diseases and procedures to be coded, preferred. 2 years related experience, preferred. At least one year experience coding one or more of the following specialties: Trauma Surgery, Orthopedic Trauma Surgery, General Surgery, Oral and Maxillofacial Surgery, Plastic Surgery.

Knowledge, Skills, and Abilities: Sound knowledge and understanding of the content of the medical record in order to be able to locate information to support or provide specificity for coding. Basic encoder skills. Knowledge of Microsoft 365 and other applicable software. Excellent communication skills, both written and verbal that present clear and concise information. Effective interpersonal, organizational, and multitasking skills. Ability to determine whether a record is complete enough to code or should be held for more documentation. Sound ability to be cooperative, dependable and responsive to the changing nature of the coding workflow. Ability to work independently and collaboratively in a fast-paced environment, managing multiple priorities with competing deadlines.

Essential Functions and Responsibilities: Codes as assigned from review of medical record documentation. Applies knowledge of current coding and billing requirements to ensure claims are submitted correctly. Monitors coding and billing performance and resolves denials related to coding errors. Performs review for charge corrections and rebilling as required for resolution of coding denials. Develops preventative measures in response to patterns identified through analysis of claims denial data; prepares periodic reports for clinical staff, identifying corrective measures to resolve denial problems. Advises and instructs providers regarding documentation and billing policies, procedures and regulations; interacts with providers regarding conflicting, ambiguous or none-specific documentation, obtaining clarification of the same. Educates providers and office staff regarding documentation coding and billing changes and regulations to assure compliance with local, state and national policies. Works collaboratively with providers, office staff, billing personnel, quality department and compliance, and coding resources to ensure accurate coding. Stays updated on coding rules, attends seminars and reviews and coding periodicals.

Decision Making: Independent judgment in planning sequence of operations and making minor decisions in a complex technical or professional field.

Working Relationships: Works directly with patients and/or customers. Works with internal and/or external customers via telephone or face to face interaction. Works with other healthcare professionals and staff.

Special Job Dimensions: None.

Supplemental Information: This document generally describes the essential functions of the job and the physical demands required to perform the job. This compilation of essential functions and physical demands is not all inclusive nor does it prohibit the assignment of additional duties.

title: Senior Analyst, Sourcing
location: (USA) Moon Pie Building AR Bentonville Home Office
description:
Position Summary...

What you'll do...

Role summary:
The Senior Analyst, Sourcing plays a critical role in supporting sourcing initiatives by managing supplier relationships, analyzing cost structures, and contributing to negotiation strategies. This position involves gathering and interpreting market data to develop sourcing proposals and collaborating with cross-functional teams to meet business objectives. The role requires strong communication and analytical skills to ensure timely decision-making and continuous improvement. The Senior Analyst also supports operational activities and fosters a culture of accountability, integrity, and excellence while driving value through strategic sourcing and supplier performance management.
About the team:
The Dairy Sourcing Team manages all Private Label supplier partnerships within the Dairy Department. This team develops and executes strategic sourcing plans by collaborating with cross-functional partners to meet product demand and quality standards. They oversee vendor relationships, negotiate contracts, and ensure cost-effective procurement aligned with company goals. The team drives innovation, monitors supplier performance, and supports continuous improvement initiatives. Members maintain high ethical standards and foster a collaborative environment that promotes professional growth and operational excellence in sourcing and supply chain management.
What you'll do:
  • Support sourcing activities aligned with the supplier matrix and contribute to cost negotiations based on component pricing insights.
  • Develop product, market, and industry knowledge to inform sourcing proposals and project execution.
  • Coordinate supplier quotations and samples to ensure timely decision-making and objective achievement.
  • Assist in planning and executing buying trips, including itinerary setup and negotiation finalization.
  • Maintain and update supplier performance data and contribute to business reports for sourcing management.
  • Collaborate with quality engineers to address supplier-related quality issues.
  • Foster strong relationships with stakeholders to support sourcing plans and continuous improvement initiatives.

What you'll bring:
  • Proven expertise in strategic sourcing and supplier management to support cost negotiations and sourcing projects.
  • Strong data analysis and financial analysis skills to develop sourcing proposals and evaluate supplier performance.
  • Effective communication abilities to build relationships with stakeholders and coordinate cross-functional activities.
  • Adaptability and a continuous learning mindset to embrace improvements and new digital tools.
  • Experience mentoring and developing associates, fostering accountability and team collaboration.
  • Commitment to integrity, compliance, and upholding company values in all business practices.

title: On-Call Room Attendant-1
description:
Mandarin Oriental, Boston is looking for an On-Call Room Attendant to join our Housekeeping team.

Are you a master of craft? Do you thrive in a team that succeeds together, demonstrating integrity and respect while acting responsibly? Do you embrace a growth mindset? We invite you to become a fan of the exceptional.

Mandarin Oriental is the award-winning owner and operator of some of the most luxurious hotels, resorts and residences located in prime destinations around the world, with a strong development pipeline. Increasingly recognised for creating some of the world’s most sought-after properties, the Group provides legendary service inspired by Asian heritage whilst representing the very cutting-edge of luxury experiences.

Mandarin Oriental, Boston is situated in the heart of the Back Bay overlooking Boylston Street. Our luxurious 138 guest rooms and 12 suites set the standard for spaciousness, combining classic Bostonian residential elegance with refined hints of our Oriental heritage. Our spa introduces Boston to world-acclaimed and unique Mandarin Oriental therapies.

About the job

Based at the Mandarin Oriental, Boston within the Housekeeping Department in Boston, Massachusetts, the Room Attendant is responsible the overall cleanliness of all guestrooms and public areas that they are assigned.

The Room Attendant reports to the Executive Housekeeper and Assistant Housekeeping Manager and receives direction from the Housekeeping Supervisors.

As On-Call Room Attendant you will be responsible for the following duties:

  • Ensure guestrooms and hotel areas are cleaned and maintained according to Mandarin Oriental Hotel Group standards and guidelines. Responsibilities include, but are not limited to:
    • Remove all soiled linens, towels, and trash from room and appropriately store or dispose of in the back of house
    • Thoroughly clean guest bathrooms using approved cleaning products
    • Organize and arrange guest amenities and personal items according to brand standards
    • Replenish amenities towels, and linens, as needed
    • Efficiently make beds to meet appearance standards
    • Clean windows and mirrors
    • Vacuum carpet and dust furniture, surfaces, vents, and baseboards
    • Report any defects in guest rooms and public areas, including damaged fixtures or furniture, for correction by the appropriate department
  • Provide exceptional service to all guests; greet guests and offer assistance, answer questions, and respond to any and all requests
  • Other duties as assigned by management

As On-Call Room Attendant, we expect from you:

  • Minimum of one year’s experience in luxury hospitality in a similar role.
  • Reading, writing, and oral proficiency in the English language.
  • Ability to stand and walk for up to 8 hours a day.
  • Ability to regularly lift, push, or pull 30 pounds.
  • Ability to work a flexible on-call schedule to include weekends and holidays.

Compensation

The hourly rate of pay for this position is $32.95.

Our commitment to you

  • Learning & Development. Your success is our success. We craft unique learning and development programmes for various stages in your career so that you grow, continuously.
  • MOstay. When you work as hard as our colleagues do, it’s important to take time off. As a member of the #MOfamily, you can stay with us wherever you go in the world. The MOstay programme offers complimentary nights and additionally attractive rates on rooms for you and your loved ones.
  • Heath & Colleague Wellness. Finding the right work-life balance is important. Your wellbeing matters to us. A variety of health benefits and wellness programmes are offered to all our colleagues, globally.
  • Retirement Plans. When you show commitment to us, we reciprocate. We offer different retirement plans depending on the length of your service and your role.

Local Benefits

  • A competitive salary and benefits packages.
  • Meals served while on duty.
  • Daily uniform laundering.
  • Discounted colleague parking available.

Equal Employer Opportunity Statement

Mandarin Oriental Hotel Group provides equal employment opportunities (EEO) to all colleagues and applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability or genetics. In addition to federal law requirements, Mandarin Oriental Hotel Group complies with applicable state and local laws governing non-discrimination in employment in every location in which the company has facilities. This policy applies to all terms and conditions of employment, including recruiting, hiring, placement, promotion, termination, layoff, recall, and transfer, leaves of absence, compensation and training.

Mandarin Oriental Hotel Group expressly prohibits any form of workplace harassment based on race, color, religion, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, national origin, age, genetic information, disability, or veteran status. Improper interference with the ability of Mandarin Oriental Hotel Group’s colleagues to perform their job duties may result in discipline up to and including discharge.

We’re Fans. Are you?

title: Payroll Specialist
description:
Who We Are

At Encore Fire Protection, we are proud to be the east coast's largest full-service fire protection company, serving over 90,000 customers from Maine to Louisiana. With a team of over 2,200 dedicated employees, we provide innovative, customized fire suppression, fire sprinkler, and fire alarm solutions that protect lives and properties every day.

Our goal is to deliver superior experience to those who trust us to safeguard what matters most. We are passionate about continuous growth, innovation, and maintaining a culture that thrives on success and commitment to safety. Our mission? To be the best fire protection company the industry has ever seen.

The Opportunity

Payroll at Encore moves quickly. With weekly payroll cycles, multiple states, unique job types, prevailing wage requirements, and a steady stream of timecards, accuracy and timing are everything. That’s why we are looking for a Payroll Specialist who enjoys staying organized, solving problems early, and keeping payroll running smoothly behind the scenes.

You will support all phases of weekly payroll across our growing workforce, ensuring codes are correct, hours are clean, laws are followed, and employees get exactly what they earned. It is a detail-heavy role that requires focus, consistency, and a knack for spotting errors that others might miss.

If you are the type of person who notices a wage code mismatch before the system even loads, or who can look at a timecard and immediately see what does not belong, you will thrive here.

Key Responsibilities

Your work centers on turning piles of timecards, wage codes, and changing rules into paychecks that are accurate the first time. You keep weekly cycles moving, catch the line that does not look quite right, and make sure people get exactly what they earned, when they expect it.

  • Process timely and accurate weekly payroll including hours collection, wage calculations, adjustments, and distribution.
  • Reconcile timecards across multiple offices and ensure hours are coded accurately and compliant with wage laws.
  • Maintain secure and accurate payroll records including rate changes, deductions, and vacation balances.
  • Serve as the go-to resource for employee payroll questions and provide timely, clear, helpful support.
  • Manage payroll deductions including benefits, garnishments, and levies.
  • Partner with HR and Finance to ensure employee data is accurate and aligned across systems.
  • Assist with identifying opportunities for process improvements, automation, and reporting enhancements.

What You Bring to the Table

  • Two to three years of payroll experience, preferably in a construction, service-based or multi-state environment.
  • Experience with certified payroll, prevailing wage, or labor-intensive payroll processes.
  • Proficiency in Excel including formulas, pivot tables, and organized data management.
  • Familiarity with payroll systems; SAGE experience is a plus.
  • Strong attention to detail with the ability to manage weekly deadlines consistently.
  • Clear and professional communication skills with the ability to support employees and internal teams.
  • A proactive, accountable mindset with the desire to improve processes and maintain high accuracy.

What Will Make You Stand Out?

  • You have a bachelor’s degree in Business, HR, Accounting, Finance, or a related field.
  • You have experience working in a private equity-backed environment or within a scaling, growth-oriented business.

#LI-LP1

Beyond the Paycheck

At Encore, we’re all about creating a culture where success is celebrated. We recognize that our work makes people’s lives safer, and we reward those who contribute to our growth. Here’s what you can expect:

  • Competitive Salary: Compensation commensurate with your experience, with the opportunity to earn performance-based bonuses tied to your personal effectiveness and contributions to the team.
  • Purpose-Driven Work Environment: We believe in working smarter, not harder. You’ll be part of a culture that values results, and we empower our team to focus on impactful work.
  • Flexible Dress Code: Upon joining, you’ll receive branded Encore swag, and we believe in dressing for the objective, not the occasion. Most days, you can enjoy a casual work environment, but we’ll dress up when the situation calls for it.
  • People-Focused Culture: We know our greatest strength is our people. That’s why we’ve built a culture that encourages experimentation, learning, and improving together. You’ll have the space to share your ideas and help shape a company that is constantly growing.
  • Tools for Success: Access to leading-edge web-based productivity tools.
  • Health and Wellness: Comprehensive medical, dental, and vision coverage to keep you and your family healthy.
  • Retirement Planning: Participation in our Fidelity 401(k) plan with a company match, helping you save for your future.
  • Life Insurance: Company-paid life insurance policy of $50,000 to give you peace of mind.

EEO Statement

Encore Fire Protection is an Equal Opportunity Employer.

Encore Fire Protection is an E-Verify Employer.

As an EEO/AA employer, Encore Fire Protection considers applicants for employment without regard to, and does not discriminate based on gender, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, race, protected veteran status, disability, or any other legally protected status.

title: (CAN) Stockperson/Cartpusher Full Time
description:
Position Summary...

As a Stockperson / Cartpusher, you play an important role in creating a safe, organized, and welcoming shopping experience for customers. You are responsible for collecting shopping carts from cart corrals, the parking lot, and the store perimeter and returning them to designated storage locations using approved procedures. Through friendly customer interactions and a strong focus on safety, this role supports smooth store operations while building valuable skills in retail, teamwork, and customer service.

What you'll do...

WHAT YOU'LL DO

Support Customer Service Operations
Collect shopping carts from cart corrals, the parking lot, and store perimeter, and return them safely to designated storage areas using Walmart approved procedures. Ensure required logs are completed accurately.

Respond to Customer and Store Needs
Respond promptly to requests from all registers and Customer Service, including assisting customers with carry outs and carry ins and verifying receipts for proof of purchase when required.

Maintain Clean Facilities and Equipment
Perform occasional cleaning and maintenance duties, including restrooms, while following established guidelines for chemical handling, proper disposal procedures, and safe use of power equipment. Complete required equipment inspections before use.

Follow Safety Standards and Procedures
Adhere to all safety and emergency protocols, including hazardous communication, lockout/tagout, and bloodborne pathogen guidelines, to maintain a safe environment for customers and associates.

Promote a Safe and Hazard Free Work Environment
Use required personal protective equipment, including steel toe footwear and a reflective vest, complete safety sweeps as required, and follow Company policies and procedures to support a clean, safe workplace.

Flexibility (Availability/Work Schedule)
• Be open to working a variety of shifts, including evenings, weekends, and holidays, based on business needs. Your adaptability helps ensure the store runs smoothly and customers receive consistent, high-quality service.

POTENTIAL CHALLENGES IN THIS ROLE

High Task Volume in a Fast-Paced Environment
• Manages overnight hours effectively and planning accordingly to stay energized and focused.
• Effectively manages work hours and plans ahead to maintain energy and focus throughout the shift.
• Handles a fast-paced workload with efficiency, ensuring large volumes of restocking and recovery are completed accurately and on time.

Physical Demands
• Performs physical tasks such as standing for extended periods, bending, and lifting inventory, equipment, or supplies weighing up to 20 lbs., and occasionally up to 50 lbs.
• Performs repetitive tasks such as stocking, zoning, and organizing products with attention to detail and consistency to uphold store standards.

WHY YOU'LL ENJOY WORKING HERE

Energetic, Fast-Paced Environment
Thrive in a dynamic setting where every day brings something new.

Appreciation for Your Efforts
Your contributions are recognized and valued.

Career Growth Opportunities
Build your skills and advance your career with us.

Meaningful Impact
Take pride in helping customers save money and live better.

The minimum rate of pay for this position starts from $17.75.

title: (USA) Staff, Software Engineer
location: (USA) Crossman Respect Building CA SUNNYVALE Home Office
description:
Position Summary...

What you'll do...

Role summary:
As a Staff Software Engineer at Walmart, you will lead the design and delivery of scalable platform capabilities, driving full lifecycle software development with a focus on cloud-native architecture and AI/ML integration. You will influence technical decisions, mentor engineering teams, and collaborate across functions to translate complex business needs into actionable solutions. This role demands a commitment to engineering excellence, innovation, and continuous improvement, ensuring high-quality, maintainable systems that support Walmart’s strategic objectives and enhance developer productivity. Your expertise will be pivotal in advancing technology and operational efficiency. **Immigration Sponsorship support will NOT be available for this position**
About the team:
The team delivers dependable software solutions by combining expertise in software development, DevOps, and solution design. Collaborating closely, members analyze requirements, design scalable architectures, and implement maintainable code using technologies such as Spring Boot. Emphasizing quality through thorough testing and continuous integration, the team manages application maintenance, performance monitoring, and troubleshooting to meet business and technical goals. Focused on continuous improvement and knowledge sharing, the team drives innovation and operational excellence in software engineering while aligning efforts with organizational objectives.
What you'll do:
  • Deliver platform capabilities including infrastructure, tooling, and ML frameworks aligned with roadmap milestones and domain priorities.
  • Drive full software development lifecycle activities such as coding, testing, deployment, monitoring, and maintenance.
  • Apply cloud-native architecture principles to ensure scalable, resilient, and agile systems.
  • Conduct system design reviews to validate feasibility and ensure quality and alignment with architectural best practices.
  • Build AI-driven applications and integrate ML components to support automation and multi-step reasoning.
  • Mentor engineers, influence technical decisions, and promote engineering excellence through documentation and knowledge sharing.
  • Collaborate across teams to define technical requirements and optimize development processes using data-driven practices.

What you'll bring:
  • Proven expertise in cloud-native architecture, including microservices, containers, and automation to ensure scalable and resilient systems.
  • Strong experience in software development lifecycle management, encompassing coding, testing, CI/CD deployment, and maintenance.
  • Proficiency in API design, software prototyping, and applying software design patterns for secure, modular, and maintainable solutions.
  • Ability to lead technical design reviews and remedial actions to uphold quality and alignment with architectural standards.
  • Skilled in DevOps engineering practices to enhance infrastructure, tooling, and developer productivity.
  • Data-driven decision-making capabilities to define metrics and optimize engineering processes.
  • Demonstrated technical leadership and mentorship fostering continuous learning and team growth.
About Walmart Global Tech
Imagine working in an environment where one line of code can make life easier for hundreds of millions of people. That's what we do at Walmart Global Tech. Were a team of software engineers, data scientists, cybersecurity experts and service professionals within the worlds leading retailer who make an epic impact and are at the forefront of the next retail disruption. People are why we innovate, and people power our innovations. We are people-led and tech-empowered. We train our team in the skillsets of the future and bring in experts like you to help us grow. We have roles for those chasing their first opportunity as well as those looking for the opportunity that will define their career. Here, you can kickstart a great career in tech, gain new skills and experience for virtually every industry, or leverage your expertise to innovate at scale, impact millions and reimagine the future of retail.

Flexible, hybrid work:
We use a hybrid way of working that is primarily in office coupled with virtual when not onsite. Our campuses serve as a hub to enhance collaboration, bring us together for purpose and deliver on business needs. This approach helps us make quicker decisions, remove location barriers across our global team and be more flexible in our personal lives.

Benefits:
Benefits: Beyond our great compensation package, you can receive incentive awards for your performance. Other great perks include 401(k) match, stock purchase plan, paid maternity and parental leave, PTO, multiple health plans, and much more.

That means understanding, respecting, and valuing unique styles, experiences, identities, ideas and opinions while being inclusive of all people.
